# Inbox Management - Control panel ⚡️ [Template page](/resources/template-page-142f6d94771f81aab191f8820bd1603d) # Inbox Management --- Efficient inbox management is crucial for booking meetings. Timely, personalized responses and organized follow-ups ensure no lead is missed, turning inquiries into opportunities. You can use either: 1. ONE Master Inbox If you have followed our [**Outreach Set Up**](https://www.notion.so/138f6d94771f816181b5e992d35848f8?pvs=21) resource you will have forwarded all emails to a master inbox 1. Your email sequencers Unibox # Time-To-Lead **TIME-TO-LEAD IS THE MOST IMPORTANT ASPECT OF INBOX MANAGEMENT - AND IS THIS SINGLE THING THAT WILL HAVE THE BIGGEST IMPACT ON YOUR CONVERSIONS** ## What is time-to-lead? Time to lead is simply the time it takes you or your inbox manager to respond back to prospects **Respond Within 5-Minutes**
⚠️ We recommend introducing a KPI for yourself or your team that means all leads are responded too within 5-minutes
## Why respond within 5-minutes? Studies have shown that businesses who respond to leads in five minutes or less are 100x more likely to connect and convert opportunities. > **Replying to prospects within 5-minutes can literally 100X your chances of converting that prospect into a meeting** > ## How To Respond Within 5-Minutes? ### Create Automations To Notify You Of A Reply
💡 We recommend setting up automations that notify you/your team of a reply from your campaigns [Fulfilment Automations](/resources/fulfilment-automations-ddb08874403448f2bf829fb6e0b1e1b9)
You can easily set up these automations within Instantly and SmartLead The notifications are INSTANT meaning it doesn’t matter if you’re not at your desk or you’re working on something else. You’ll receive the notification and then you or your team can get back to the prospect within the 5-minute KPI **Here’s how the automation looks:** ![IMG_0708733A555C-1.jpeg](Inbox%20Management/IMG_0708733A555C-1.jpeg) ### Setting Time-To-Lead KPI for Your Inbox Manager As we’ve said throughout this resource we recommend having the 5-min time-to-lead KPI Here’s what we recommend to encourage your inbox manager to achieve this KPI: - Pay your inbox managers per booking - Implement the KPI = <5-min time to lead - Tell them this KPI will 100x their bookings and therefore increase the amount they will earn - Put them in the Slack channel where the reply notification is received - They're now incentivised to hit KPI and earn more # Inbox Management Basics The GOAL of inbox management is to get a “YES” or “NO” from a prospect - If you get a “YES”, you schedule a call - If you get a “NO”, you move on ## Do’s and Don’ts Of Inbox Management ### ✅ Do - [ ] Respond to replies within 5-minutes - [ ] Book a call manually instead of using Calendly links - [ ] Be creative with your follow ups. Mention your case studies. Break down the ROI of working with you assuming average numbers - [ ] Follow up on LinkedIn too, leave them a voicemail or call them - [ ] Record a loom where you show your face and display their website or email exchange and tie in what you say to the ROI they’d get and personalise it as much as possible ### ⛔️ Don't - [ ] Leave it hours before you reply - [ ] Send Calendly links - [ ] Give up with the follow ups until you get a clear “YES” or “NO” - [ ] Just use phrases like “bumping this up” or “circling back” - [ ] Use just email to follow up - [ ] Share the pricing details - [ ] Explain your offer in-depth # Managing Your Inbox and Follow Ups
⚠️ **REMEMBER - TIME KILLS ALL DEALS** - Keep the following in mind Throughout the time in-between when a lead says they’re interested and when a call is booked, the lead can lose interest. > The more time a lead has to lose interest, the more likely they will lose interest > The less time a lead has to lose interest, the less likely they will lose interest > The longer the time between responses, the less likely they will respond again. > If you respond back quickly, then the lead may in turn respond to you quickly.
### **1. The goal is to book sales calls, NOT sell the product/service over email** Don’t give prospects all the details, just intrigue them enough to want to find out more. The only job right now is to get to the call. It’s a step-by-step approach. ### **2. Never give out prices over email, prices are only discussed during sales calls** Rarely does a prospect become a client if they’re given a price before a call. They are shopping on price, and we never try to be ‘the cheapest option’. The major reason is that the prospect only understands the cost, but they don’t understand the full benefits. We cannot properly convey the benefits of our service and nullify objections in an email, it must be done on a call. If the prospects refuses to book a call without hearing a price you can give them a range, or an explanation of pricing, without giving the actual amount ### **3. Respond as quickly as possible** Just do it. ### **4. Keep your messages brief** Some emails will need to be longer than others, but every email should be as short as possible. 50 words or less is a good amount. If you find a sentence that is unneeded, remove it. ### **5. You and them are equals** We are not excited, privileged, lucky, inferior, or nervous in our tone. Doesn’t matter if it’s a 100 Million/year CEO or a simple marketing manager. We are contacting them to offer them something that could benefit their business. Nothing more, nothing less. ### **6. Be professional-casual with your tone** 70% Assertive / To the point 30% Friendly / Polite ### **7. Never stop following up until you get a Yes or a No** To convert the most leads possible, we have to continue to message them until we get a clear answer. Either they say Yes to moving to the next stage (booking a call, signing a contract, etc), or we get a definite No.
💡 Review our lead nurturing section for instances of when and how to follow up as well as templates to use [Lead Nurturing](/resources/lead-nurturing-aac29f87fb5f4d76b6b6751aefbe3fce)
### 8. Sending Looms As Follow Ups **Looms are incredibly effective in securing meetings** They allow you to give details on how you would benefit the prospect and their business, and talk about your relevant social proof. They also build a connection between you and the prospect, as they see your face and hear your voice. Most people don’t send looms. The ones that do usually send are using a non-effective framework that wastes time & effort. ****Below is the exact framework used to gain turn leads in sales calls, which allows you to turn them into closed clients / new contracts. **Loom Video Framework Breakdown:** **1. Value Prop / Intro** - Talk about what your offer is and any risk reversal/guarantee. Who you are, who you service, what results you generate 1. **About Us** - Talk a little bit about yourself and your business. What you’ve done, the clients you’ve served, results you’ve got, your team and how long you’ve been running. - Create a story that they follow along & feel a connection with what you are saying. People buy stuff from people they like, that’s just a fact. - Put some humour in there and be genuine - **Note:** Do not focus too much time on this part, this is to build a connection and humanise you. But the majority of your time and content should be structured around them. 1. **Social Proof** - Put your money where your mouth is. - Explain that your business can do what you say because you guys have done it before, and multiple times repeatedly. - Show & explain past client results. - Compare these results to something that you can do for them. - Tell them how your clients felt about these results, tell them how you got these and what you did differently for each to get these results. Just make it like a story again 1. **Position The Outcome - The Desired End State** - *SHOW/EXPLAIN to them what you would do for them…but don’t give away the sauce!* - **Run them through a very brief plan of what you’d do for them SPECIFICALLY, but do not tell them how you’d do it.** - Simple is best. Don’t confuse them. It needs to be very clear and easy to understand what you’d do for them. Make it seem like they could do this on their own, but due to them not having the time or experience it wouldn’t have nearly the same effect as if they outsourced it to you. - For example, let’s say you’re selling SEO and the lead runs a home renovations business. Show them how they are ranking in their city for “kitchen remodel” or “bathroom remodel”. Tell them you could get them to rank higher for those high-quality terms, which will directly result in more people calling the lead for those services. 1. **Value Prop Again & CTA** - Drop the hammer here again. Talk about you offer again because now everything you just said before leads them to believe that your offer is legit because of every statement you talked about. - All of this information, you are leading them through a chain of beliefs to the final point where you say the offer one last time & end with a CTA. ⚠️ **AS SOON AS THEY VIEW IT YOU NEED TO REPLY** ⚠️ Right when they view the video, you need to follow up & simply just ask what they think (*You will get an email from Loom saying someone has watched your video - note that you only get a notification for the 1st time someone watches)* Give them 5-10 minutes in case they watched and are writing a reply. But if it’s been 10 minutes with no reply, you need to message them. - “What were your thoughts on the video?” - “Saw you just watched the video, what did you think?” - “See you just watched the video, any thoughts John?” Of course, you could also try calling them after viewing the video. Keep it simple, what matters most here is just following up as soon as you see them view the loom # Calling Leads To Follow Up Hands down the most effective thing is to just call the lead. Even if you’re going to send over a Loom video, send some other content or document, or just type a reply, calling them immediately builds a connection and establishes rapport. They will remember who you are much better, and be much more receptive to anything you say or send afterward. ## 1. Finding their phone number If the lead has their phone number in their email signature, or if you think the number on their website or google business page will get you to your lead, call it. **How to find their phone number:** - In their email signature - On their website - Google their business and ask to be put through to them - reference they’ve just emailed you ## 2. What do I say? If you get someone else (a gatekeeper, assistant, other employee, their wife, etc): The tricks here are to: 1. Refer to the fact the lead has been emailing you 2. Sound confident and in a bit of a rush to speak to the lead - **Example** Gatekeeper 👩: Hi, this is ABC Company, Sandra speaking You 😀: Yeah hi Sandra, I’m looking to talk to John please 👩: John’s not in right now, may I ask who’s calling? 😀: This is Name from Company , John and I were just emailing each other about a business partnership, can you put me through to him or his voicemail If the gatekeeper refuses to put you through, just tell them who you are and why you called, and that the lead can call you back if they want. *(Don’t get mad at them if they block you - it’s their job to do this)* **Once you’re speaking to the prospect** Just introduce yourself, tell them you wanted to touch base and then tell them what you’re going to do next (send a video, a document, etc). Getting them on the phone and hearing your voice & tone is the primary goal. This helps build a small connection and they will remember you better. If you get a sense they are open or receptive, you can spend some time talking about your service and/or setting up a sales call time. Even if you’re sending something for them to review beforehand, locking in a call is a great idea. - **Examples** “Hey John, I’m Travis, you just sent me an email about _____(info on something or wanting to arrange a call). “Just wanted to reach out before I send the video over, I’ll send it in the next X minutes/hours ## 3. Leaving A Voicemail If they don’t answer, leave a voicemail. I probably wouldn’t call again (at least not soon, you may be calling them days or weeks later if they’ve gone silent on you). Leaving a voicemail still gives them a bit of a connection to you, they’ll hear your voice and tone, and still remember you better. - **Example** “Hey John, this is Name, you just messaged me about _____. Was just calling to let you know I’ll send you a video about _____, probably in the next hour or two. Call me back at 123-456-7890 if you want.” # Sending Digital Assets It’s always a good idea to send prospects assets that provide more context about how you can help and demonstrate social proof 👇 **Check out our Sales and Marketing Asset Templates** [Sales & Marketing Assets](/resources/sales-marketing-assets-b8bc52a597034733862d71617324cbfd) # Response Templates We’re always looking to turn cold email responses into opportunities. Here’s a structured guide to help you navigate various responses and maximise your chances of converting leads. **Find more templates here:** [Email Template Vault](/resources/email-template-vault-9bec1049170b45f890306b0cce46250f) ## **Typical Prospect Replies**
❓ **Prospect:** Can you send more information?
✅ **Our recommended responses:** Thanks for reaching out and showing interest in [Your Company/Service/Product]. I appreciate your request for more information and would love the opportunity to dive deeper into how we can specifically help you achieve [outcome]. To be able to provide relevant and tailored information and to be able to give you real insights into if we could help, a short call would be the most efficient way to do this to understand what informaiton you’re looking for. This will NOT be a sales call and is our way of providing you the informaiton you need to be able to understand if we’re worth continuing to talk to or not. Are you free for 15-minutes tomorrow at [time]
❓ **Prospect:** What’s the price?
✅ **Our recommended responses:** Our p*ricing varies from $X to $Y* Whilst I appreciate it can be frustrating, please give us some flexibility as our service is modular and we don’t have an off the shelf service, meaning we don’t have a fixed price. I’ve attached our process and [sales asset/ideally an ROI calculator]. If you’re looking to solve [problem] and can share what you need, I can give you a price straight away. Are you free for 15-minutes tomorrow, So I can give you a price?
❓ **Prospect:** It’s not my responsibility. Speak to X
✅ **Our recommended responses:** Thank you for kindly passing on the relevant contact. **Proceed to then email the contact they provided** **Email to new contact:** **Subject:** {prior prospect} suggested I contact you **Email:** Hi X, {prior prospect} suggested I contact you. (Rest of email body as per initial email)
❓ **Prospect:** I don’t see how your service is different from others we’ve tried.
✅ **Our recommended responses:** I completely understand where you're coming from and appreciate your honesty. It’s crucial to find a solution that not only meets your needs but also stands out from what you've tried in the past. Here’s what sets us apart: 1. **[Unique Value Proposition]** 2. **[Proof of Success/Case Studies]** 3. **[Customisation and Support]** Are you against giving us 30-minutes on [day & time] to show you how we’re different and can actually solve [problem]?
❓ **Prospect:** We need to see examples of your work or references from similar clients before we even look at this
✅ **Our recommended responses:** I have attached our case study deck for you to review. We have worked with [xyz] similar clients however, they haven’t volunteered to be contacted and so we can not share their information. [more social proof] [unique value proposition] We believe in being transparent and building trust from the start. Are you against giving us 30-minutes on [date & time] to demonstrate how we can solve [problem] for [company name]?

# Lead Nurturing - Control panel ⚡️ [Template page](/resources/template-page-142f6d94771f81b08e00fabb1bccd246) # Lead Nurturing ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE FOR $30K/MONTH SYSTEM USERS:** If you're reading this off the back of your **Re-Engagement Campaign**, good, you’re exactly where you need to be.
## Why Lead Nurturing Matters --- Most founders think nurturing = spam. **But, nurturing is how you compound attention.** You don’t need 10K new leads this month. You need 40 of the right ones to stay warm until they’re ready to buy. ## How to Set Up a Lead Nurturing System --- You’re not going to remember who to follow up with. And even if you do, you won’t do it manually. That’s why we recommend setting up **automated lead nurturing campaigns** so no lead goes completley cold just because you forgot. So here’s the system we recommend: ### The Lead Nurture Campaign Structure --- - **Instance-Based Follow-Ups**: 5 tailored emails based on the lead's current stage (e.g. ghosted after call, “send more info”, didn’t show) - **Seinfeld Nurture Campaign**: If they don’t respond to those 5 → push them into a long-term Seinfeld-style sequence (10–20 emails, 14–30 days apart) This system means: - You don’t rely on memory - You keep leads warm over months - You close deals others would’ve lost # When To Follow-Up We recommend creating automated campaigns to follow-up in these instances. **PLEASE NOTE** - If sending information or answering a question etc in reply to their initial response, this is done manually, the follow-ups are automated to make sure we get a reply from them ### Instances: 1. **Pre-Talking Stage** 1. No response to initial campaign 2. **Engaged Prospects** 1. Expressed interest or need convincing 3. **Pre-Call Stage** 1. Booked a call but didn't show 2. Showed interest but didn’t book 3. Expressed future interest 4. **Post-Call Stage** 1. Positive call but no response 2. Need to speak with someone else to make decision 3. Requested more info 5. **Re-engagement of Previously Interested Leads**: 1. Nurturing previously interested leads to re-engage # **What Should Your Follow-Ups Look Like?** ## **Pre-Talking Stage: Didn't Respond to Initial Campaign** **Approach**: Transition them into a less salesy, more value-driven sequence. - **Email 1**: Share additional information or insights relevant to their business needs - **Email 2**: Address a common pain point and how your solution can help - **Email 3**: Provide a case study demonstrating ROI and success - **Email 4**: Offer industry insights or trends that could impact their business - **Email 5**: Introduce a lead magnet, such as a free guide, video, or provide value for free (free leads If no response push them into the lead nurturing sequence ## **Engaged Prospects** ### **A. Expressed Interest, Sent More Info** **Approach**: Nudge them towards a decision. - **Email 1**: "Just checking in – any thoughts on the info we sent?" - **Email 2**: "Can I answer any questions about our last email?" - **Email 3**: "Here's a quick recap of [Service/Benefit] - Drive ROI." - **Email 4**: "I thought this additional insight might be helpful [new info/link]." - **Email 5**: "Hard focus on ROI + Hard CTA - Ask if something want to look at further down the line" ### **B. Needs Convincing, Sent More Info** **Approach**: Reduce perceived risk. - **Email 1**: "Discover how [Your Service] helped [Similar Company] succeed." - **Email 2**: "Understanding the ROI – a closer look at what we offer + Case Study." - **Email 3**: "Hearing from our clients: [Client Testimonial]." - **Email 4**: "Are these challenges familiar? Here’s how we can help." - **Email 5**: "Our guarantee + scarcity offer" Again, If no response push them into the lead nurturing sequence ## **Pre-Call Stage** ### **A. Booked a Call But Didn't Show** **Approach**: Get them to reschedule. - **Email 1**: "We still haven’t heard – is everything okay?" - **Email 2**: "Let’s find a new time that works for you [Reschedule Link]." - **Email 3**: "Is there a better way to connect?" - **Email 4**: "Is this no longer a priority for you?" - **Email 5**: "ROI + Case Study share" ### **B. Interested in Booking But Didn’t Book** **Approach**: Encourage booking a call. - **Email 1**: "Need help scheduling our call? Let’s make it easy." - **Email 2**: "Here’s a bit more about what you can expect from our call - Pre-call guide." - **Email 3**: "Wondering about [Service/Benefit]? Here’s the specifics" - **Email 4**: "A special offer for those that book [Incentive/Resource/Discount]." - **Email 5**: "Your FAQs – ready when you are." ### **C. Interest Expressed for Future Consideration** - This instance is dependant upon the time-frame, we recommend here putting them straight into the lead nurturing sequence # **Lead Nurturing Email Sequences:** The goal of this sequence is to stay at the top of their mind and to provide actionable value that brings benefits that makes them feel they have to work with you. **Objective**: To engage leads monthly over 10 months, offering actionable lead generation insights and tips. ## **10-Email Seinfeld Sequence for Lead Generation Services** **Month 1 - The Lead Gen Landscape:** - Subject: "Navigating the Evolving World of Lead Generation" - Content: Discuss current trends in lead generation and what it means for businesses **Month 2 - The Insightful Tip:** - Subject: "One Simple Change to Improve Your Lead Quality Today" - Content: Offer a practical tip or strategy to enhance lead quality **Month 3 - The Success Story**: - Subject: "How We Helped [Client Name] Skyrocket Their Leads" - Content: Share a case study or success story illustrating the impact of effective lead generation but a challenge that client had to overcome **Month 4 - The Tech Talk:** - Subject: "Top Lead Generation Tools Every Business Should Know" - Content: Introduce and explain some useful tools or tech in lead generation - Don’t mention things like Apollo **Month 5 - The Strategy Deep-Dive**: - Subject: "Rethinking Your Lead Funnel: Strategies That Work" - Content: Outline overviews of advanced strategies or approaches to lead generation **Month 6 - The Industry Focus**: - Subject: "Lead Generation Secrets in [Industry]" - Content: Tailor insights to a specific industry, showing deep understanding and expertise **Month 7 - The Free Strategy Call**: - Subject: "Want a free strategy call?" - Content: Offer a free call to develop a strategy for them based on what you’ve shared **Month 8 - How To Rethink Your Offer for Lead Gen**: - Subject: "Is your offer ready for outreach?” - Content: Share insights on how to re think they’re offer for lead gen **Month 9 - The Quick Wins**: - Subject: "5 Quick Wins in Lead Generation for Immediate Results" - Content: Provide easily implementable tips for quick improvements in lead generation **Month 10 - The Reflective Wrap-Up**: - Subject: "A Year of sharing" - Content: Book a call ## **5 Email Nurturing Sequence** This is a 5 email lead nurturing sequence that lasts 8 weeks with emails being sent 14 days apart Our examples below are for: B2B SaaS client that provides loyalty programs Use this as a template to build a nurturing campaign around your offer (certain aspects have been removed to maintain privacy) - **Email 1** > **Subject:** {{contact.company_name}}’s loyalty program > --- > **Email Body:** > --- Hi {{contact.first_name}}, My name's {Name} from {Company Name}. We help brands collect GDPR compliant, 1st party data, direct from end consumers using our powerful loyalty platform. **What are loyalty programs?** Loyalty programs are systems set up by companies to elevate customer engagement, improve retention and increase customer lifetime value. It is a marketing strategy that aims to develop a stronger relationship between a customer and a brand with the objectives of repeat sales, increasing profits, and brand equity. **Who needs a loyalty program and why?** It’s well known that it costs 5-10x more to acquire a new customer than to retain an existing one. So you could say that every company needs one, right? There are plenty of retention strategies out there besides loyalty programs. However, not all repeat customers will become your **brand advocates**. This is what loyalty programs are so great at. If done well, pushing the customer up the loyalty ladder ultimately leads to advocacy, the **holy tier of customer loyalty**. I have attached a more detailed resource for you to review. If this is something you'd be interested in implementing for {{contact.company_name}}, happy to arrange a quick chat to go through how we can help. Kind regards, {Name} (attach supportive material to the email if you have it such as Decks, brochures, guides, etc.) - **Email 2** > **Subject:** 7 ways to increase {{contact.company_name}}’s customer loyalty > --- > **Email Body:** > --- Hi {{contact.first_name}}, Here are 7 ways to increase customer loyalty to {{contact.company_name}}: **1 - Understanding customers** You need to know what customers value and what they need in order to build a relationship with them. **2 - Communication** To build a relationship with a customer you need personalized communications **3 - Engagement** To be engaged, customers need to be entertained by your content. It’s important that they want to engage with your communications **4 - Feedback** Reviews, continued analysis of these reviews, developing new products and conflict handling are all ways that feedback can be helpful. **5 - Customer service** Bad customer service can turn away a customer from a trusted brand forever. The importance of having great customer service can not be overstated. **6 - Brand identity** It’s important to determine what your brand stands for and what value you provide for your customers. **7 - Loyalty program** An incredibly useful part of having your own loyalty program is the ability to track the progress of customers and their transactions. If you’d like to know more about loyalty programs and how we can help {{contact.company_name}}, please feel free to let me know. Kind regards, {Name} (attach supportive material to the email if you have it such as Decks, brochures, guides, etc.) - **Email 3** > **Subject:** 6 things a good loyalty program does > --- > **Email Body:** > --- Hi {{contact.first_name}}, In my last email, I went over 7 ways to increase customer loyalty to {{contact.company_name}}. I'd now like to share with you 6 things a good loyalty program should do: **1 - Encourage repeat customers** **2 - Create a bond with customers** **3 - Go beyond transactions** **4 - Turn data into insights** **5 - Allow personalized actions** **6 - Be flexible** If you’re interested in understanding how our loyalty platform can do this for {{contact.company_name}}, I would love to arrange a time to have a quick chat. Kind regards, {Name} (attach supportive material to the email if you have it such as Decks, brochures, guides, etc.) - **Email 4** > **Subject:** Top 8 ways to reward loyal customers > --- > **Email Body:** > --- Hi {{contact.first_name}}, Rewarding loyal customers is a great way to build brand loyalty and turn customers into referrers. Here are the top 8 ways to reward your loyal customers: **1 - Offer unique access** **2 - Gamified rewards** **3 - Loyalty tiers** **4 - Partnership rewards** **5 - Referral programs** **6 - Reward customer data** **7 - Make them the star** **8 - A surprise with an order** I have attached a document that goes into this in more detail. Are these things you'd like to implement for {{contact.company_name}}'s customers? Kind regards, {Name} (attach supportive material to the email if you have it such as Decks, brochures, guides, etc.) - **Email 5** > **Subject:** {{contact.company_name}} + {Company Name} > --- > **Email Body:** > --- Hi {{contact.first_name}}, Over the past couple of months, I have highlighted the importance of having a loyalty program and how {Company Name} can implement such a program for {{contact.company_name}}. Having recently created bespoke loyalty programs for - {List of clients worked with} I would love to talk about how we could do this for {{contact.company_name}} too. Do you have 15-minutes this week or next to discuss this further? Kind regards, {Name} ← Previous [Running & Improving Campaigns](/resources/running-improving-campaigns-23f0565975a9488b95c3e96ebb32a3bc) Next → [Inbox Management](/resources/inbox-management-75e840029b8f41f092760aec9ccf6cc1) On this page

# Segmentation & Personalisation - Control panel ⚡️ # Segmentation & Personalisation --- **Did You Know?** Personalised emails can boost reply rates by up to 6x (Experian) ### **The Problem** The art of personalisation goes beyond just inserting {{FirstName}} in your email. Real personalisation is about creating specific messaging that resonates with prospects and sets out that you understand their position on a deeper level than most. ### **Go Beyond Basic Personalisation** Your lead sheets are a goldmine of information. The more details data you have, the more personalised a emails can be. **So how do you personalise emails? You want to use things like:** - [ ] {{FirstName}} - [ ] {{CompanyName}} - [ ] {{JobTitle}} - [ ] {{Country}} - [ ] {{City}} - [ ] {{TechStack}} This allows you to use placeholders to better personalise your emails at scale. So instead of a generic email like this: --- > “Hi {{FistName}}, saw that you guys are a marketing agency and so wondering if you needed leads?” > You can send a much more personalised email like: --- > “Hi {{FirstName}}, as the {{JobTitle}} at {{CompanyName}}, I noticed your team in {{City}} has been expanding. Are you finding it challenging to generate consistent leads?” >
⚠️ **You have to make sure that you format these placeholders in your lead sheet correctly to be able to use them in your emails. This can take time but is absolutely worth it You can use [LeadFormatter](http://leadformatter.com) to automate this**
**Checklist To Use When Formatting Your Lead Sheet. Make sure:** - [ ] There are no BLANK cells for first names - [ ] All leads have an email address - [ ] There are no duplicate emails - [ ] There are no BLANK cells for company names - [ ] That company names are formatted correctly and don’t include links, special characters, emojis or anything else that wouldn’t look like you personally wrote their correct company name ### **How To Use GPTSheets To Create Personalised Icebreakers** --- [https://www.loom.com/share/068e13d008f74329ac6a00eb2a9e497a?sid=6e5039a6-69c0-44a2-96c9-44fd442006cd](https://www.loom.com/share/068e13d008f74329ac6a00eb2a9e497a?sid=6e5039a6-69c0-44a2-96c9-44fd442006cd) ### Using First Lines Although all tests we have done show first-lines DON’T improve the performance of our email, we know people like to use them We actually found they work better as a P.S so test that vs as a ‘first-line’ ### **Use 👉 [Quicklines](https://www.quicklines.ai) to get first lines** **How to use it** [https://cdn.embedly.com/widgets/media.html?src=https%3A%2F%2Ffast.wistia.net%2Fembed%2Fiframe%2F9da0fv8jsp&display_name=Wistia%2C%2BInc.&url=https%3A%2F%2Fsamuel-thompson.wistia.com%2Fmedias%2F9da0fv8jsp&image=https%3A%2F%2Fembed-ssl.wistia.com%2Fdeliveries%2F24a8687808b85522b2c5b4cd8e45b0eb81d55b6f.jpg%3Fimage_crop_resized%3D960x568&key=96f1f04c5f4143bcb0f2e68c87d65feb&type=text%2Fhtml&schema=wistia&wvideo=9da0fv8jsp%22%3E%3Cimg](https://cdn.embedly.com/widgets/media.html?src=https%3A%2F%2Ffast.wistia.net%2Fembed%2Fiframe%2F9da0fv8jsp&display_name=Wistia%2C%2BInc.&url=https%3A%2F%2Fsamuel-thompson.wistia.com%2Fmedias%2F9da0fv8jsp&image=https%3A%2F%2Fembed-ssl.wistia.com%2Fdeliveries%2F24a8687808b85522b2c5b4cd8e45b0eb81d55b6f.jpg%3Fimage_crop_resized%3D960x568&key=96f1f04c5f4143bcb0f2e68c87d65feb&type=text%2Fhtml&schema=wistia&wvideo=9da0fv8jsp%22%3E%3Cimg) ### **Making your emails more personal** Your biggest advantage when sending emails is your **personality**, this is how you **stand out** from everyone else! Don’t be boring, or monotonous, inject your personality and some humour. Remember, the person you’re contacting will likely get 5-10 similar emails every day! **A great tip I read is, write like you’re TIPSY** Truly personalised emails come from: - Targeting - Research - Segmenting ## Using ChatGPT To Come Up With Angles Whilst in this example our member Travis uses this to find better angles for his emails when contacting Roofing companies This can be applied to any industry and is a great way to come up with a fresh approach ### Watch This Video [https://youtu.be/QpYm-xKXCBY](https://youtu.be/QpYm-xKXCBY) ← Previous [Writing Emails](/resources/writing-emails-9bc75337ac584d679d465712848be02c) Next → [Presenting Your Offer](/resources/presenting-your-offer-09f8204f21cd4b1fb07e9d755208813e) On this page

cold-email/writing-emails-9bc75337ac584d.content:
# Writing Emails - Control panel ⚡️ [Template page](/resources/template-page-142f6d94771f81b5be05f1f054041153) # Writing Emails --- ## 1. What is a cold email? A cold email is an unsolicited e-mail that is sent to a receiver without prior contact. Because of this, there is a structure we should follow to capture prospects’ attention, create curiosity, credibility and get them to take action.
💡 Click [here](https://script.google.com/macros/s/AKfycbztM7BEH7eYC4WZYHrlodevfqN0x6DsdYRFSkw_6TgXqd1uphI4ZtLaGWUqjWkQ2Jt3/exec) to access our email writing tool
## 2. The Ever-Evolving Landscape of Cold Emails In 2020 there were 306.4 billion emails sent every day (Statista), and this number is only going up. It is expected that 376.4 billion emails will be sent EVERYDAY by 2025. A significant portion of these are cold emails, making the competition fierce. **The Generic Email Epidemic** A survey by Convince & Convert found that people receive an average of 147 emails a day, but they only open 30% and click through just 3.2%. Meaning we have to do more to stand out. Because of this, the old way of sending cold emails just isn’t working. You can’t just use a {{FirstLine}} and book a meeting. **The Need for Evolution** Cold emailing isn't dead; it's just evolved. With the increasing competition, the old tactics no longer yield the same results. To succeed in today's landscape, it's crucial to adapt and innovate ## 3. Understanding the Fundamentals The fundamentals of writing a cold email will ALWAYS outperform looking for a quick hack or a shiny object that seems to fix everything. Get these right and you will get results: ### 1. Relevance This is probably the most overlooked fundamental principle of cold email How do we make our emails relevant? We do this by understanding our target market, their pain-points, solutions they’ve already tried to solve it and their other options available to them Deep market research and segmenting lead lists dramatically helps make our emails more relevant ### 2. Length A cold email body with more than 4 sentences (150 words) see 15x average response rate as other types Understand your emails are an interruption in your prospects day - so they need to be able to scan through them quickly and determine if they really want to read it. Shoot for MAX 200 words, but ideally shorter. ### 3. Subject Lines Your subject line is the **first thing your prospect sees**—and it determines whether your email gets opened or ignored. Personalised subject lines get 35% more opens Make sure to always include personalisation. - Subject Lines Best Practices **Do:** - Keep it **short and relevant** (5-7 words). - Personalize when possible (e.g., use their name or company). - Spark curiosity without being clickbait (e.g., "Quick idea for [Company]"). - Focus on the recipient's **pain point or benefit** (e.g., "Boost your [specific metric]"). **Don't:** - Avoid **spammy language** like "Free," "Urgent," or "Act Now." - Don’t overuse caps or exclamation marks (e.g., "SAVE BIG TODAY!!!"). - Don’t mislead—stay **authentic and transparent**. - Consider avoiding generic phrases like "Quick question" - Subject Examples: - {{FirstName}}, thoughts? - {{CompanyName}} x {{Your company}} - {{FirstName}}, question about {{CompanyName}} - {{FirstName}}, question about {{PainPoint}} - Question for {{FirstName}} - Ideas for {{CompanyName}} - {{Service}} chat - Chat about {{Service}} ### 4. Focus on Outcome and Offer In your initial cold email, the goal is to spark **curiosity and intrigue**. Diving into details about how you deliver your service often falls flat and fails to grab attention. Instead, focus on **communicating the outcome** you can achieve and the value the prospect will gain. **Example:** *"We can add $650,000 to your pipeline in 83 days."*
⚠️ If you’re targeting sophisticated markets with a saturated offer (paid ads to ecom), you might see better results by including your service/mechanism and what makes it unique. We recommend you split test this.
### 5. Personalisation People often think personalisation meanings using first-lines etc. These have been proven time and time again not to work. You truly make an email personalised by making it super targeted, relevant and by using personalised data points such as: - {{Firstname}} - {{Companyname}} - {{Industry}} - {{Title}} - {{Technology}} - {{Funding}} ### 6. Call to Actions A Call-to-Action (CTA) is how you guide your prospect to take the next step. The right CTA depends on your goal and the stage of the relationship. **Types of CTAs:** - **Hard CTAs**: - Direct and action-focused (e.g., "Book a call here"). - Best for warm leads or those ready to act. - **Soft CTAs**: - Low-pressure, encouraging dialogue (e.g., "Does this sound like something you'd consider?"). - Ideal for early outreach to spark a response. - **Value/Interest-Based CTAs**: - Focus on offering value (e.g., "Would you like to see how we added $500K to a pipeline in 3 months?"). - Value/Interest-based CTAs have a **30% success rate**, making them twice as effective as other types of CTAs. Whatever you decide to to, make sure to use ONE call to action that is SUPER CLEAR to the prospects ### 7. Testing and Optimisation MarketingExperiments found that A/B testing of email campaigns led to a 127% increase in conversions for the winning version.
⚠️ **ALWAYS BE TESTING YOUR EMAILS**
## 5. Cold Email Structure Each section of a cold email is designed to guide the prospect through a journey from curiosity to action. Now that you understand the fundamentals, you can better understand what each section of your cold email should do: ### 1. Subject Line - **Purpose**: Get them to open the email - **Strategy**: Creating a subject line that sparks enough curiosity to get them to open the email. Making a subject line as close to an internal email as possible always works well ### 2. First Sentence - **Purpose**: Get them to read the next section - **Approach**: Highlighting that you’re aware of a pain point they have or sparking curiosity for them to continue to read ### 3. Email Body - **Purpose**: Push them to the call-to-action - **Content**: Here you show you’re aware of their potential problems, the solutions they’ve tried to solve them, demonstrated your creditability and highlighted how you may be able to help ### 4. CTA - **Purpose**: Get them to take action - **Execution**: End with a CLEAR CTA. Asking a question works better than asking directly for a call as does asking if you can send them something. ## 6. Frameworks and Approaches ### 1. Feynman Technique Framework 1. **Choose a Concept:** Select a topic or concept that you want to understand or learn more about. 2. **Pretend to Teach:** Imagine you are teaching this concept to someone else who has no knowledge of the subject. This step is crucial as it requires you to break down the concept into simple terms and explanations. 3. **Identify Gaps:** As you explain the concept, pay attention to any areas where you struggle to convey the information clearly. These gaps indicate areas where your own understanding may be incomplete or unclear. 4. **Review and Simplify:** Go back to the original material and review it again. Look for ways to simplify and clarify your explanations. Use plain language, analogies, and relatable examples to help illustrate the key points. 5. **Teach Again:** Repeat the process by teaching the concept again, but this time focusing on addressing the gaps and refining your explanations. Strive to make your teaching as clear, concise, and easy to understand as possible. - **Using this approach to write cold emails** ### **Using this approach to write cold emails:** --- **Step 1: Choose A Concept:** Writing cold emails **Step 2: Pretend to Teach:** Imagine you are explaining your/your clients service to someone who has no prior knowledge. Break down the concept of the service into simple terms and explain it step by step. Focus on the benefits and outcomes your service can provide for the recipient's business. **Step 3: Identify Gaps and Weaknesses:** Critically review your explanation and identify any areas that may be unclear or confusing.Pinpoint weaknesses in your message around the offer and address them to enhance your understanding. **Step 4: Review and Simplify** Go back to your original cold email draft and review it with a fresh perspective. Simplify the language, eliminate jargon, and ensure clarity in your message. Write it so a 12 year old could understand it and shorten it **Step 5: Teach Again** Rewrite your cold email using the insights gained from the previous steps. Focus on addressing the gaps and weaknesses you identified earlier. Craft a clear, compelling, and concise message that highlights the value of your service for the recipient's business. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/0a53601b409344c4981fda9dd9918e6c](https://www.loom.com/share/0a53601b409344c4981fda9dd9918e6c) ### 2. REPLY Framework The REPLY framework consists of: R- Relevant results E- Empathy P- Personalisation L- Laser-focus Y- You (prospect) oriented - REPLY Framework Example ```jsx Hi {{FirstName}}, would [results] benefit {{Companyname}}? We understand [pain points] and often [indsutry] have tried [past solutions] If {{Companyname}} could [solve pain point] would that [desired result]? CTA ``` ### 3. Question-Based Approach The Question-Based Approach consists of: 1. Using a simple questions as a way to get people to reply and get out foot in the door 2. We also get more information from them that lets us qualify them better 3. We usually ask them if they’re having a problem with X/experiencing a pain point 4. If they do, that’s perfect. It gives us a smooth entry for the pitch. - Question-Based Approach Example ```jsx Hi {firstname},Quick question. Are you experiencing {pain point}? CTA ``` ### 4. Reaching Out Because… The thinking behind this approach is simple; when you email someone who don’t know you, how do you start? You tell them THE REASON you’re emailing them. So to mimic that, we’re going to do the same thing here. A short greeting and immediately a reason why you’re emailing them. It’s natural and people expect it. - **Reaching Out Because Example** ```jsx Hi {{FirstName}}, We’re reaching out because of {{the specific ‘problem’ found}}. Here’s how {{use your offer to solve that problem}} CTA ``` ## 7. Common Mistakes To Avoid
⚠️ **Relying on Templates:** - **The Trap:** Falling into the comfort zone of using generic, widely available templates. If you’re using them, thousands of others are too.
⚠️ **Ignoring Personalisation:** - **The Trap:** Sending the same email to your entire list, disregarding individual preferences or needs.
⚠️ **Neglecting Subject Lines:** - **The Trap:** Treating subject lines as an afterthought, relying on “Quick question” to save the day.
⚠️ **Overwhelming with Content:** - **The Trap:** Packing too much information into an email, making it overwhelming or confusing for prospects - The best cold emails are 200 words or under.
⚠️ **Ignoring Mobile Optimisation:** - **The Trap:** Not thinking how your email looks on mobile devices, where a significant portion of emails are read.
⚠️ **Neglecting Testing and Analytics:** - **The Trap:** Sending emails without monitoring their performance or understanding what’s working.
⚠️ **Failing to Segment Your List:** - **The Trap:** Treating all your contacts as if they have the same needs and interests.
⚠️ **Ignoring Email Frequency and Timing:** - **The Trap:** Bombarding your audience with too many emails or sending them at the wrong time.
⚠️ **Tracking Opens & Clicks:** - **The Trap:** Whilst its nice to be able to see these analytics, tracking opens and clicks can have a negative impact on deliverability.
## 8. Spintax Spintax is an easy way to automatically add variation to your email scripts. When sending emails, you want to slightly tweak the messaging/wording so that the same message isn't continuously being sent to a ton of different people and therefore being sent to the spam folder **How Spintax Helps Improve Your Campaigns** Spintax makes every script sent out seem unique without you actually having to write a separate email every single time. Email service providers can easily spot when you send the exact same email to every lead which can hurt your inbox rates **When To Use Spintax** A mistake many people make is considering using spintax as the same as split-testing. It’s important to test different offers, angles, Unique selling points and messaging. Use spintax once you have a script that's working well and you’re looking to scale. That way you can send similar variations of a working script without hurting your deliverability. - **How To Use Spintax In Instantly** 1. **How To Setup Spintax In Instantly.ai** [https://www.loom.com/share/ebce7fd4f769480686d901eba80c75c0](https://www.loom.com/share/ebce7fd4f769480686d901eba80c75c0) 1. **Understanding Spintax In Instantly** In Instantly spintax is initiated by using two {{ to open spintax and two }} to close spintax. You must use “RANDOM” within the spintax when using in Instantly. You can’t include custom variables within the spintax **Example**: *Can I {{RADNOM|send it to you|show it to you}}?* 1. **Spintax Templates** 1. **Greetings/Opener to output “**Hey James, Good afternoon and happy Wednesday.”
{{{RANDOM|Hi|Hey|Hello}} {{firstname}}, Good {% assign time_h = 'now' | date: '%H%M' %} {% if time_h < 1200 %}morning {% elsif time_h < 1500 %}afternoon {% else %}evening {% endif %} and happy {# "now" | date: "%A" #}.
2. Sign Off to output “Regards,”
{{RANDOM|Thanks|Cheers|Best|Regards}},
3. Opt-Out to output: “If you’d prefer to not have any future emails from me, no problem, just respond back “don’t need this”
If {{RANDOM|you would|you'd}} {{RANDOM|rather|prefer to}} not {{RANDOM|have|receive|get}} any {{RANDOM|more|new|other|additional|future}} {{RANDOM|emails|messages|follow ups}} from me, no {{RANDOM|worries|problem|problemo|hard feelings}}, just {{RANDOM|respond|hit me|reply|message}} back "{{RANDOM|not for me|no thanks|not interested|no thank you|don’t need this}}
- **How To Use Spintax In Smartlead** 1. **Get Started in Smartlead** [https://www.loom.com/share/12ae350f43db4f17a1ef6a8a7095e452?sid=332a8cb6-4f2b-456d-bd68-5906828df137](https://www.loom.com/share/12ae350f43db4f17a1ef6a8a7095e452?sid=332a8cb6-4f2b-456d-bd68-5906828df137) **2. Understanding Spintax In SmartLead** In SmartLead spintax is initiated by using one { to open spintax and one } to close spintax You can include custom variables within the spintax **Example:** {Can we show you how we can help|Can we show you the demo we created for {{Company Name}}?} Would produce: - Can we show you how we can help {{Company Name}} - Can we show you the demo we created for {{Company Name}}? 1. **Spintax Templates** 1. **Greetings/Opener**
{Hello|Hi|Hey}
{{ sl_day_of_week }} This would output the current day of the week so you can say “Happy Monday for example”
{{ sl_time_of_day }} This would output the current time of the day and would output either: Morning, Afternoon or Evening
{{sl_date “now_wd 1Days”}} This would produce a day, a set number of **working days** from the day the email was sent. You can change the number of days by replacing the number in this section: 1Days” This would allow you to automate saying: Are you free Monday 21st May
b. Thanks and Opt Out:
{|Thanks|Cheers|Best|Regards},
If {you would|you'd} {rather|prefer to} not {have|receive|get} any {more|new|other|additional|future} {emails|messages|follow ups} from me, no {worries|problem|problemo|hard feelings}, just {respond|hit me|reply|message} back "{not for me|no thanks|not interested|no thank you|don’t need this}”
💡 **Once you have gone through these resources, the single biggest thing that will impact your ability to write better emails is testing and feedback. Make sure to use our #messaging-review channel in our Slack to get feedback on your emails.**

For outreach, we suggest a minimum of 12 domains. We **only** recommend using these domain endings: .com .co Domain shouldn't have any numbers or hypens (-) **Never** use the domain that’s hosting your website for outreach. If your main domain is [**business.com](http://business.com)** → you could get domains as shown in the examples below ### Burner Domains Example - [ ] appbusiness.com - [ ] trybusiness.com - [ ] emailbusiness.com - [ ] mailbusiness.com - [ ] getbusiness.com - [ ] gobusiness.com - [ ] growbusiness.com - [ ] mybusiness.com - [ ] runbusiness.com - [ ] demobusiness.com - [ ] csbusiness.com - [ ] salesbusiness.com # Making it easier to find domain variations Watch this video and use the spreadsheet we created to create tons of domain variations. - Video [https://www.loom.com/share/96c1f807dc6a4f4789c37ef0d737d119?sid=0f854d07-8156-4a68-97b5-654b19b8fc04](https://www.loom.com/share/96c1f807dc6a4f4789c37ef0d737d119?sid=0f854d07-8156-4a68-97b5-654b19b8fc04) - Spreadsheet
[**Sheet Link**](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1QnM1LI7gyYXMbkKO2yGCjmiUQ6C5I-gGRMZU4Kci0EI/edit?usp=sharing)
# How many inboxes should I set up? We **recommend** only 1 inbox per domain. Don’t go above 2 inboxes per domain. # Buying domains We’ve created step-by-step guides for purchasing domains through three different providers.
⚠️ In this day and age, most decent resellers will set up your inboxes for you and connect them to your email sequencer. If you go with a reseller, simply fill out their form, give them access to your domain provider and they will handle the rest.
Choose your preferred provider and follow the guide. **Important:** - Ensure each account has a unique Google Workspace subscription with only one email account per workspace. - Similarly, each account should have its own Microsoft 365 subscription, with one email account per subscription. # Add DMARC to your domain (do this for ALL domains) **DMARC value to copy:** ``` v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:name@domain.com ``` Here instead of “name@domain.com” you just put your main email address. Any existing email address, whether it’s an @gmail address or a domain-based address, would work. The process is the same for any domain provider. Simply input the same TXT record in the same way in your preferred domain provider - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/5210b746e8fd4d06b91b8cef5328f869?sid=9f6b9400-27e6-4a8a-8a47-61fc98556d52](https://www.loom.com/share/5210b746e8fd4d06b91b8cef5328f869?sid=9f6b9400-27e6-4a8a-8a47-61fc98556d52) # How to activate your Google Workspace account, log in to Gmail and forward all the emails to the main inbox
⚠️ **You need to choose one of the 12 inboxes dedicated for outreach to be the “MAIN” one and have all the emails from the other inboxes be forwarded to that “MAIN” one. Then you can reply to all emails using just that one inbox.**
Setting up forwarding is meant to help you manage all of your emails in one inbox so you don't have to keep switching between accounts. This is particularly important as you add more and more inboxes that need to be managed. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/046d62988e8545d983272695270f0608](https://www.loom.com/share/046d62988e8545d983272695270f0608) # Change the profile picture in Google Workspace This will show you how to allow members of your company to edit their own profile pictures as well as how to setup profile photos for your outreach accounts. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/65cb6f20757c458995c871e19497d12f](https://www.loom.com/share/65cb6f20757c458995c871e19497d12f) # How to set up a Gmail Spam Filter This is to make sure your prospect’s replies don't end up going into your spam folder. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/6856789bc3404e009b56c8274f084ced](https://www.loom.com/share/6856789bc3404e009b56c8274f084ced) # Setting Up 2 Factor Authentication Make sure to enable “Per-User Outbound Gateways” before executing this step. Find It Here - [Allow Per-User Outbound Gateways](https://admin.google.com/ac/apps/gmail/enduseraccess?hl=en) - Video [https://www.loom.com/share/e16a17ed56dc473499b1f89d2bea8f32?sid=aad0c8b6-3aa6-4809-8c24-808c86495d3d](https://www.loom.com/share/e16a17ed56dc473499b1f89d2bea8f32?sid=aad0c8b6-3aa6-4809-8c24-808c86495d3d) # Set up send-as functionality and use just one inbox to send from all your domains We set up forwarding so that you can have all your prospects’ replies in one inbox. This will enable you to reply to all emails you receive from the same inbox but your replies will all be from the appropriate account and in the same email thread to keep everything aligned. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/38c9deb4cbe24e19bc41896155126d01?sid=87f6deeb-e0a2-4e3e-9030-0bfbfe1fef90](https://www.loom.com/share/38c9deb4cbe24e19bc41896155126d01?sid=87f6deeb-e0a2-4e3e-9030-0bfbfe1fef90) - SOP Walkthrough for video [https://app.tango.us/app/workflow/How-to--Send-As--from-the-Master-email-box-6787bb8d0809462baa823947e34d9568](https://app.tango.us/app/workflow/How-to--Send-As--from-the-Master-email-box-6787bb8d0809462baa823947e34d9568) # F**ilter out DMARC reports from your inbox** Frequent DMARC reports from all 12 inboxes being forwarded into your main inbox can make things messy very quickly. Here’s an easy way to automatically handle DMARC reporting. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/fd0774862f2e440faa383710e0262074](https://www.loom.com/share/fd0774862f2e440faa383710e0262074) ### **DMARC senders to filter out:** --- - noreply-dmarc-support@google.com - dmarc_reports@reports.emailsrvr.com - noreply-dmarc@zoho.com - dmarcreport@microsoft.com - abuse@seznam.cz # Remember To Do This Separately For All Domains… You should have 12 different workspace subscriptions with one email account per subscription
If you have any questions around DNS click here 👇 [DNS FAQs](../../Key%20Resources/Frequently%20Asked%20Questions%20(FAQs)/DNS%20FAQs%20142f6d94771f819fb5f5de5b8032678a.md)

# Connect Your Accounts To Instantly/Smartlead # Connect Your Accounts To Instantly/Smartlead --- ## Connect Domains To Instantly - Connect Domains To Instantly ## 1. Make sure you have an Instantly account **Sign Up Here: [Instantly](https://instantly.ai/?via=sean)**
🎁 Use discount code ‘PEPE’ for 10% off every month 2: Connect each domain to Instantly
## 2: Connect each domain to Instantly [https://www.loom.com/share/d4f5ba794e644fa5a0bb0634aa922099](https://www.loom.com/share/d4f5ba794e644fa5a0bb0634aa922099) ## 3. Add Domain Custom Tracking Link Always make sure to setup your own custom tracking link for every domain [https://www.loom.com/embed/f48659965fea458eb16ba1ddb5018135](https://www.loom.com/embed/f48659965fea458eb16ba1ddb5018135) ## Step 4: Warm your domains
⚠️ **We recommend that you ONLY warm your domains for 4 days. Stop warmup after this.** **See [Recommended Settings](/resources/key-resources/recommended-settings) for all current warmup and sending settings.**
[https://www.loom.com/share/f05c5adc93bc4e13bf2efa4b6d24550d?sid=cf1006f1-e85c-4993-ade0-30ec5a89f6b8](https://www.loom.com/share/f05c5adc93bc4e13bf2efa4b6d24550d?sid=cf1006f1-e85c-4993-ade0-30ec5a89f6b8)
💡 Remember to ramp up your campaigns after warmup using the schedule below. You can do this by increasing the number of new leads so the right number of emails go out each day per inbox depending on the number of inboxes in the campaign - *Days 1 - 3:* **5 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 4 - 6:* **10 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 6 - 9:* **15 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 10 - 12:* **20 emails a da**y **per inbox** - *Days 13+:* **25 emails a day per inbox**
## 5. F**ilter warm-up emails in your inbox (needed only in your main inbox)** ---
👇 This is to keep your inbox clean so you don't end up missing any prospect responses
[https://www.loom.com/embed/7df36906a2cc45118edaf05b017f9a99](https://www.loom.com/embed/7df36906a2cc45118edaf05b017f9a99) ## Connect Domains To SmartLead - Connect Domains To SmartLead ## 1: Make sure you have a SmartLead account Sign up here: [Smartlead](https://smartlead.ai/?via=sean-longden) ## 2: Connect each domain to SmartLead [https://youtu.be/gd-XrG0BX-4](https://youtu.be/gd-XrG0BX-4) ## Step 3. Add Domain Custom Tracking Link
💡 Always make sure to setup your own custom tracking link for every domain
[https://youtu.be/LMg7xOMisuw](https://youtu.be/LMg7xOMisuw) ## Step 4: Warm your domains
⚠️ **We recommend that you ONLY warm your domains for 4 days. Stop warmup after this.** **See [Recommended Settings](/resources/key-resources/recommended-settings) for all current warmup and sending settings.**
[https://youtu.be/H61EKxHuL4c](https://youtu.be/H61EKxHuL4c)
💡 Remember to ramp up your campaigns after warmup using the schedule below. You can do this by increasing the number of new leads so the right number of emails go out each day per inbox depending on the number of inboxes in the campaign - *Days 1 - 3:* **5 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 4 - 6:* **10 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 6 - 9:* **15 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 10 - 12:* **20 emails a da**y **per inbox** - *Days 13+:* **25 emails a day per inbox**
## Step 5. F**ilter warm-up emails in your inbox (needed only in your main inbox)** This is to keep your inbox clean so you don't end up missing any prospect responses ## Troubleshooting Issues - **Google Workspace Disconnects** **What to do when your Google Workspace Account disconnects from Instantly or SmartLead (App Password Connection)** - Think if you’ve recently changed your password (That may be the cause) - Whether or not you have though, Go inside Google and create a new app password. - Wait a few hours and then re-add the account to Instantly/SmartLead with the new app password. - **Google locks your account** If you get this: ![Untitled](../../Key%20Resources/Frequently%20Asked%20Questions%20(FAQs)/Inbox%20&%20Deliverability%20FAQs/Untitled.png) - Log in as you normally would - Lower your sending volume - See if you got any very negative replies - If you did, make sure to edit your email script accordingly If asked for number verification, you can get a US-based number from [textverified.com](http://textverified.com) or smspva.com. Skype and Hushed numbers do not work for these one-time verification requests. It needs to be a sim-based number. - Google doesn’t let you use a phone number from Hushed to set up 2FA Use a Skype number - What if I want to give my credentials for software to my VA? - Generally if the program is known to kick you out for that, Don’t do it - If you absolutely need to, set up a remote desktop or have them use a VPN to your country (ideally city) - If the program is fine with sharing credentials, use a Password Manager like Bitwarden - Get backup codes to Gmail accounts if you have 2FA set up? - Generally, it’s a good idea to get your codes every time for every account with 2FA - If you lose the phone number you won’t be able to get the account back not even with Google’s support. - You can find the backup codes option in Gmail account settings (Manage your account → Security), under 2-Step Verification - I can’t access my Google account, how can I get back into it? You need to go through the Google account recovery process👇 [Recovery](https://toolbox.googleapps.com/apps/recovery/domain_in_use?visit_id=638296504607098933-4249281426&rd=1) ← Previous [Outlook Setup](/resources/outlook-outreach-set-up-1bc4184f233043b7b1469164bc05a883) Next → [Campaign Setup](/resources/campaign-set-up-dde64e0a0815402ea10aec9473f4a15e) On this page

# Outlook Outreach Set Up - Control panel ⚡️ [Template page](/resources/template-page-142f6d94771f81c19b13dba6e54984c1) # Outlook Outreach Set Up --- For your own work email, we recommend using Google Workspace. For outreach, 50% of your inboxes should be Outlook. Follow this guide to set up outreach using Outlook. # How many domains should you buy? Buy as many as you’d like. We suggest a minimum of 12 domains. We **only** recommend using these domain endings: .com .co Domain shouldn't have any numbers or hypens (-) **Never** use the domain that’s hosting your website for outreach. If your main domain is [**business.com](http://business.com)** → you could get domains as shown in the examples below ### Burner Domains Example - [ ] appbusiness.com - [ ] trybusiness.com - [ ] emailbusiness.com - [ ] mailbusiness.com - [ ] getbusiness.com - [ ] gobusiness.com - [ ] growbusiness.com - [ ] mybusiness.com - [ ] runbusiness.com - [ ] demobusiness.com - [ ] csbusiness.com - [ ] salesbusiness.com # Making it easier to find domain variations Watch this video and use the spreadsheet we created to create tons of domain variations. - Video [https://www.loom.com/share/96c1f807dc6a4f4789c37ef0d737d119?sid=0f854d07-8156-4a68-97b5-654b19b8fc04](https://www.loom.com/share/96c1f807dc6a4f4789c37ef0d737d119?sid=0f854d07-8156-4a68-97b5-654b19b8fc04) - Spreadsheet
[**Sheet Link**](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1QnM1LI7gyYXMbkKO2yGCjmiUQ6C5I-gGRMZU4Kci0EI/edit?usp=sharing)
# Check Availability & Buy Domains **You can buy domains from:** - GoDaddy - Porkbun - Squarespace If you want to buy domains in bulk, search on google "godaddy bulk domain buy" or "porkbun bulk domain buy”. Purchase the domains for 1 year only and it should cost you $10 - $13 per domain. # Setup Domain Forwarding How To Setup URL Forwarding In Porkbun In Bulk. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/85825d57aab248f3aa2fe31c3614674a?sid=1e6fb11c-5be1-4871-bac0-0f8b0ef71702](https://www.loom.com/share/85825d57aab248f3aa2fe31c3614674a?sid=1e6fb11c-5be1-4871-bac0-0f8b0ef71702) # 5. (Optional) Enabling API Access Turning On API Access is necessary if you would like to be able to setup DNS records for Outlook automatically in the future through Porkbun. If this is only a one-time setup for you, this step is not necessary. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/6a42bdc8e7154585abab189b2dcf9649?sid=ab09c9af-2019-4551-989d-16f9ba24b966](https://www.loom.com/share/6a42bdc8e7154585abab189b2dcf9649?sid=ab09c9af-2019-4551-989d-16f9ba24b966) # Set Your Microsoft Admin Panel
⚠️ In this day and age, most decent resellers will set up your inboxes for you and connect them to your email sequencer. If you go with a reseller, simply fill out their form, give them access to your domain provider and they will handle the rest.
To set up your Microsoft Admin Pane, go to this [link](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/business/compare-all-microsoft-365-business-products). Add no more that 20 emails per admin account/panel. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/d3b7c404957348dcb2c71494194fed34?sid=6411ec19-b4bb-45c9-992d-b2d67c92f548](https://www.loom.com/share/d3b7c404957348dcb2c71494194fed34?sid=6411ec19-b4bb-45c9-992d-b2d67c92f548) # Setup Your Domain Backend ### **Add Domains & Setup Accounts In Admin Panel**
💡 Adding DNS records is the same for each domain registrar (Porkbun, Godaddy, Squarespace, etc). Input the same records into the same fields just on your preferred domain registrar
- We will add the domain and all necessary records to ensure good deliverability. - Make sure to enable DKIM 20 to 30 minutes after adding it to your DNS records. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/17dc3fb8b21245e18da4fbc8b7382a12?sid=9c5397ea-6e45-4682-ac45-83b6bbb4a695](https://www.loom.com/share/17dc3fb8b21245e18da4fbc8b7382a12?sid=9c5397ea-6e45-4682-ac45-83b6bbb4a695) # Setup Your Master Inbox - Go to https://security.microsoft.com/antispam - Make sure you have logged in to the admin panel email which ends with 'onmicrosoft.com'. - **Enable the forwarding** - it should show options for Yes and No. Click on Yes - Enable the forwarding again, and if it shows an error, try once more and it should work. If not, please wait for 3 hours and try again. - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/169c67a6df9541018652e2da0ba42f5b?sid=642bffec-f26f-4e41-a29c-672a3223337e](https://www.loom.com/share/169c67a6df9541018652e2da0ba42f5b?sid=642bffec-f26f-4e41-a29c-672a3223337e) ### How To Set Up Send-As Between Google & Outlook (No App Password Needed) 1. **Modify Security Defaults** 1. Go to the Azure Portal: https://portal.azure.com/ 2. Log in as organization Admin. 3. Navigate to Azure Active Directory > Tenant Properties. 4. Click on Manage security defaults at the bottom of the page. 5. Set Enable security defaults to No. 6. Click Save. 2. **Enable SMTP AUTH for Your Account**
💡 **This step is optional but if something doesn’t work you want to follow these steps:**
1. **Enable SMTP AUTH at the Tenant Level (if disabled)** 1. Go to the Microsoft 365 Admin Center: admin.microsoft.com 2. Navigate to Settings > Org settings > Modern authentication. 3. Ensure Authenticated SMTP is enabled. 2. **Enable SMTP AUTH for the User** 1. In the Admin Center, go to Users > Active users. 2. Select your user account. 3. Under Mail, click Manage email apps. 4. Ensure that Authenticated SMTP is checked. 5. Click Save changes. # Connect mailbox to your Google Master Inbox 1. **Open Gmail Settings:** 1. Log in to your Gmail account. 2. Click the **gear icon** in the upper-right corner and select **See all settings**. 2. **Go to Accounts and Import:** 1. Click on the **Accounts and Import** tab. 3. **Add Another Email Address:** 1. Under the **Send mail as** section, click **Add another email address**. 4. **Enter Your Office 365 Email Details:** 1. **Name:** Enter the name you want to display. 2. **Email Address:** Enter your Office 365 email address. 3. Uncheck **Treat as an alias** if you want replies to go to your Office 365 inbox. 4. Click **Next Step**. 5. **Configure SMTP Server Settings:** 1. **SMTP Server:** smtp.office365.com 2. **Port:** 587 3. **Username:** Your Office 365 email address. 4. **Password:** Your Office 365 password or the app password if you created one. 5. **Secured connection using TLS.** 6. Click **Add Account**. 6. **Verify Your Email Address:** 7. **Gmail will send a verification email to your Office 365 mailbox.** 8. **Log in to your Office 365 email and click the verification link or enter the confirmation code in Gmail.** # Set Your Profile Picture - Go to https://admin.microsoft.com/#/users - Make sure you're logged in with your admin panel email address. (The one that ends in .onmicrosoft.com) - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/d20342165d9d495caa363794d046975e?sid=a3dcfe9d-f24a-493e-a3d8-5f9b8bbe3a2e](https://www.loom.com/share/d20342165d9d495caa363794d046975e?sid=a3dcfe9d-f24a-493e-a3d8-5f9b8bbe3a2e) # Remove Recurring Verification Codes When you log in to the Outlook account, you might receive a prompt saying, "Your organization requires additional security information. Follow the prompts to download and set up the Microsoft Authenticator app” We want to avoid this, so we will disable the security features from our admin panel. Go to: https://entra.microsoft.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_IAM/TenantOverview.ReactView *Sometimes Microsoft re-enables this security feature after about a month, so login to the admin panel and do the steps again.* - **Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/72cb6510df054041b6ad92c927ea0464?sid=700c1909-321b-4777-ba77-6e52562c56a4](https://www.loom.com/share/72cb6510df054041b6ad92c927ea0464?sid=700c1909-321b-4777-ba77-6e52562c56a4) # Automate Future DNS Setup **BONUS:** Add All DNS Records Using Automation
💡 If you do this automation, you can skip step 7 above in the future. Still, be sure to do everything else.
> **Copy the sheet from [here](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1fKGqpPC5JiAlw1dcP8yByIuS6roXFe4VBDiD0KBRYfM/edit?usp=sharing)** > ### **Download the blueprint** [Bulk add dns records in porkbun.json](Outlook%20Outreach%20Set%20Up/Bluk_add_dns_records_in_porkbun.json) - **Set Up Video Part 1** [https://www.loom.com/share/8548af133fba4ee8a6734d5ef9958b77?sid=92137954-ff3f-4ba6-881b-e98e92d67929](https://www.loom.com/share/8548af133fba4ee8a6734d5ef9958b77?sid=92137954-ff3f-4ba6-881b-e98e92d67929) - **Set Up Video Part 2** [https://www.loom.com/share/50f45bcde0c14dbaa399bbde9461173b?sid=3215e12e-bdee-4303-9dab-6a175e86edc7](https://www.loom.com/share/50f45bcde0c14dbaa399bbde9461173b?sid=3215e12e-bdee-4303-9dab-6a175e86edc7) # Automate Future DNS Setup If you get an error like this: ![Screen-Shot-2023-11-16-at-5.43.46-pm.png](Outlook%20Outreach%20Set%20Up/Screen-Shot-2023-11-16-at-5.43.46-pm.png) Clear the cache of the browser and try it again. If you get any other error, try clearing the cache & it might work.
💡 If you have any questions around DNS click here 👇 [DNS FAQs](../../Key%20Resources/Frequently%20Asked%20Questions%20(FAQs)/DNS%20FAQs%20142f6d94771f819fb5f5de5b8032678a.md)

# Recommended Software # Recommended Software Use the resources below to identify your ideal tech stack. These are tools we’ve personally tested and rely on daily—not recommendations driven by affiliate partnerships.
💡 **Here’s Our Recommended Software Stack** | **Use Case** | **Recommendation** | | --- | --- | | Email Sending + Warming | Instantly | | LinkedIn | Expandi | | Call Scheduling | Calendly | | CRM | Close | | Lead Scraping | Apollo (Agencies, SaaS & Ecom) & Clientscraper (Local Businesses & Linkedin) | | Lead Verifying | Millionverifier & Scrubby (Catch-Alls) | | Email Deliverability | EmailBison |
## Software Discount Section [Make sure to use our discounts](/resources/exclusive-offers-discounts-72d58984acc14abca9f9e55bbc2653a0) # Full Breakdown ### Email Sending + Warming: --- - [**Instantly**](https://instantly.ai/?via=sean) - [**Link**](https://instantly.ai/?via=sean) --- - [**Smartlead**](https://smartlead.ai/?via=sean-longden) - [**Link**](https://smartlead.ai/?via=sean-longden) --- ### LinkedIn Software: --- - [**Expandi**](https://expandi.io?fp_ref=sean95&fp_sid=ccgroup)
ℹ️ **Good old software with all the functions you need**
- [**Link**](https://expandi.io?fp_ref=sean95&fp_sid=ccgroup) --- - [**LIA**](https://getlia.io/)
ℹ️ Very similar software to Expandi, choose the one that looks better to you
- [**Link**](https://getlia.io/) --- ### Call Scheduling Software: --- - Calendly
ℹ️ Let’s you create a booking link to your calendar for people to book a call with you when you’re avilable
- [**Link**](http://calendly.com) --- ### CRM: --- - GoHighLevel
ℹ️ All-around solution to manage your clients and campaigns
[**Link**](http://letsgohighlevel.com) --- - Close CRM
ℹ️ Close CRM is probably for you if you want to move deals forward quickly by getting on the phone with interested prospects and scheduling calls directly as opposed to sending email and waiting for a reply. Here’s an overview of the platform
[https://www.loom.com/share/54e9ad4dc8e9439ca27aad0d14eb26b3?sid=3e68fb0d-1305-46ff-91d0-325dfc20c823](https://www.loom.com/share/54e9ad4dc8e9439ca27aad0d14eb26b3?sid=3e68fb0d-1305-46ff-91d0-325dfc20c823) [Link](http://letsgohighlevel.com) --- ### Lead Scraping Software: --- - ClientScraper + sales nav
ℹ️ This is essentially KleanLeads/IcyLeads but at 50% off for the group

# Setting Up Incogniton # Setting Up Incogniton --- Use Incogniton to prevent your accounts from getting flagged. Make sure to use Incogniton to create separate profiles and setup/manage your accounts on one proxy per client. Open Incogniton [Here](https://incogniton.com/)
💡 If you use Unibox in your sequencer, Incogniton is not necessary.
# How To Set Up Incognition [https://vimeo.com/981791560](https://vimeo.com/981791560) # How to Structure Incognition - Create one Incogniton browser profile attached to one proxy - Use that to setup all your accounts for 1 client as you normally would - Log into that browser profile through Incogniton to manage responses in your master inbox ← Previous [Software Stack](/resources/recommended-software-142f6d94771f81778969d55dca76865b) Next → [Buy Domains + Set up Google Workspace/Outlook](/resources/buy-domains-set-up-google-workspace-a924182697eb4ed9b216e02925a83b72) On this page

# Cold Outreach Templates - Control panel ⚡️ [Template page](/resources/template-page-15af6d94771f811cb7a1c875f2227aa0) # Cold Outreach Templates --- ## 1. Initial Contact with a New Prospect
✉️ **Subject:** Thoughts, {{FirstName}}? **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, I noticed that {{Company}} has been making strides in the {{Tech}} space. At [Your Company], we specialize in helping businesses like yours achieve [specific outcome]. We recently helped {{Case study}} increase their [relevant metric] by [percentage/result]. I believe we could help {{Company}} achieve similar results. Can we schedule a brief call to discuss how we can support your goals? Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 2. Reaching Out to Warm Leads
✉️ **Subject:** Quick Question, {{FirstName}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, It was great speaking with you at [event/previous interaction]. I wanted to follow up on our conversation about [topic]. At [Your Company], we have been able to help clients like {{Case study}} achieve [specific outcome]. I believe there’s a lot we could do for {{Company}} as well. Are you available for a call next week to explore this further? Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 3. Following Up on a Previous Meeting or Interaction
✉️ **Subject:** Next Steps, {{FirstName}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, I enjoyed our conversation about {{Company}}'s goals and challenges in the {{Tech}} space. As discussed, I’m sharing more details on how we helped {{Case study}} achieve [specific outcome]. Can we set up a follow-up meeting to dive deeper into how we can help {{Company}} achieve similar results? Looking forward to your thoughts. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 4. Introducing Your Service to a New Market
✉️ **Subject:** {{Company}}'s Growth **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, I see that {{Company}} is expanding its presence in the {{Country}} market. At [Your Company], we’ve helped businesses like {{Case study}} navigate new markets and achieve [specific outcome]. I’d love to share some insights on how we can support {{Company}} in {{Country}}. Could we schedule a brief call next week? Best regards, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 5. Networking at Industry Events
✉️ **Subject:** Great to Meet You, {{FirstName}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, It was a pleasure meeting you at [Event]. I was impressed by {{Company}}'s work in the {{Tech}} space. At [Your Company], we specialize in [service/product] and have helped clients like {{Case study}} achieve [specific outcome]. I’d love to continue our conversation and explore how we might collaborate. Are you available for a coffee chat next week? Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 6. Requesting a Referral from a Mutual Connection
✉️ **Subject:** Introduction Request, {{FirstName}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, I hope you're well. Our mutual connection, [Mutual Connection's Name], mentioned that you might be interested in [specific outcome]. At [Your Company], we specialize in helping businesses achieve just that. We’ve worked with clients like {{Case study}} to deliver [specific outcome]. I’d love to discuss how we might be able to support {{Company}} as well. Can we schedule a quick call next week? Best regards, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 7. Cold Outreach to Decision-Makers in Specific Industries
✉️ **Subject:** {{FirstName}}, {{Industry}} Growth **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, I see that {{Company}} is a key player in the {{Industry}} industry. At [Your Company], we’ve helped businesses like {{Case study}} achieve [specific outcome] with our [service/product]. I believe we could help {{Company}} enhance its performance and achieve similar results. Can we set up a brief call next week to discuss this? Looking forward to your response. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
