# Pitching Templates - Control panel ⚡️ [Template page](/resources/template-page-15af6d94771f81e09505cd2976d63923) # Pitching Templates --- ## 1. Initial Pitch to a New Client
✉️ **Subject:** Opportunity for {{Company}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, I noticed that {{Company}} is focused on [specific area or goal]. At [Your Company], we help businesses like yours achieve [specific outcome] through [brief description of your service/product]. For instance, we helped {{Case study}} increase their [relevant metric] by [percentage/result]. I believe we could achieve similar results for {{Company}}. Could we schedule a brief call to explore this opportunity further? Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 2. Follow-Up Pitch After an Initial Meeting
✉️ **Subject:** Next Steps for {{Company}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, It was great meeting with you to discuss {{Company}}'s goals. Based on our conversation, I wanted to outline how [Your Company] can help you achieve [specific outcome]. Our approach has helped clients like {{Case study}} see [specific result], and I’m confident we can deliver similar success for {{Company}}. Let’s schedule a follow-up meeting to discuss the next steps. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 3. Detailed Proposal Submission
✉️ **Subject:** Proposal for {{Company}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, Attached is the detailed proposal we discussed for helping {{Company}} achieve [specific outcome]. The proposal outlines our approach, timeline, and expected results. Our track record with clients like {{Case study}} demonstrates our ability to deliver [specific result]. Please review the proposal and let me know if you have any questions or if we can schedule a time to discuss it further. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 4. Pitching a New Idea to an Existing Client
✉️ **Subject:** New Idea for {{Company}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, Given our ongoing work with {{Company}}, I wanted to propose a new idea that could help you achieve [specific outcome]. By implementing [brief description of the idea], we could enhance [specific aspect] and drive [specific result]. Our experience with {{Case study}} showed impressive results, and I believe we can replicate this success for {{Company}}. Can we discuss this idea in more detail at your convenience? Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 5. Requesting a Meeting to Discuss Opportunities
✉️ **Subject:** Exploring Opportunities for {{Company}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, I’d love to schedule a meeting to discuss potential opportunities for {{Company}} to achieve [specific outcome]. At [Your Company], we specialize in [brief description of your services/products], which have helped clients like {{Case study}} achieve [specific result]. Could we find a time next week to discuss how we can collaborate to reach {{Company}}’s goals? Looking forward to your response. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 6. Presenting a Custom Solution to a Prospect
✉️ **Subject:** Custom Solution for {{Company}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, Based on our discussions and understanding of {{Company}}'s needs, I have developed a custom solution designed to achieve [specific outcome]. This solution leverages [brief description of key features or approaches]. Our client {{Case study}} saw [specific result] with a similar approach, and I believe we can deliver similar success for {{Company}}. Can we schedule a call to go over the details and address any questions you might have? Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 7. Responding to an RFP (Request for Proposal)
✉️ **Subject:** Proposal for {{Company}}'s RFP **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, Thank you for the opportunity to respond to {{Company}}'s RFP for [specific project or need]. Attached is our detailed proposal, which outlines our approach to achieving [specific outcome], along with our timeline and pricing. Our proven track record with clients like {{Case study}} demonstrates our ability to deliver [specific result]. Please review the proposal and let me know if you have any questions or if we can arrange a call to discuss further. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]

# Response Handling Templates - Control panel ⚡️ [Template page](/resources/template-page-15af6d94771f81c9826bee8f777c30e9) # Response Handling Templates --- ## 1. Responding to "Not Interested”
✉️ **Subject:** Thanks for Your Feedback, {{FirstName}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, Thank you for letting me know. I understand that {{Company}} might not be looking for [specific service] right now. If circumstances change or if there’s another way we can support {{Company}}, please don't hesitate to reach out. We’ve helped clients like {{Case study}} achieve [specific outcome] and would love the opportunity to do the same for you. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 2. Responding to "Already Using a Service”
✉️ **Subject:** {{Same as initial message}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, That’s great to hear that {{Company}} is already using [current service]. May I ask when was the last time you did a cost and service comparison to ensure you're getting the best value? At [Your Company], we offer a complimentary cost and service comparison to help businesses like yours make sure they’re receiving optimal benefits. Our clients, including {{Case study}}, have found this comparison extremely insightful and have achieved [specific outcome] as a result. Would you be open to a brief call to discuss this and see how we might enhance your current setup? Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 3. Responding to "Send More Information”
✉️ **Subject:** {{Same as initial message}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, I’d be happy to provide more information about how [Your Company] can help {{Company}} achieve [specific outcome]. Attached is a detailed overview of our [service/product], including case studies and results from clients like {{Case study}}. Let me know if you have any questions or if you’d like to schedule a call to discuss this further. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 4. Responding to "Pricing Inquiry”
✉️ **Subject:** {{Same as initial message}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, Thank you for your interest in our services. We can provide a ballpark range of [price range] and assure you that you will receive a minimum ROI of [specific ROI]. However, our pricing is modular and depends on your specific needs and requirements. Could we schedule a call to discuss your needs in more detail and provide you with an exact cost? Looking forward to your response. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 5. Responding to "Scheduling a Call”
✉️ **Subject:** {{Same as initial message}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, Great to hear that you’re interested in scheduling a call. How about [suggest two possible dates and times]? During the call, we can discuss how [Your Company] can help {{Company}} achieve [specific outcome], drawing from our success with clients like {{Case study}}. Looking forward to speaking with you. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 6. Handling "No Budget" Objections
✉️ **Subject:** {{Same as initial message}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, I understand that budget constraints are a concern for {{Company}}. We’ve worked with clients like {{Case study}} to deliver significant value while staying within budget. Could we discuss potential options that might fit your current financial planning and still help you achieve [specific outcome]? Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]
## 7. Handling "Contact Later" Responses
✉️ **Subject:** {{Same as initial message}} **Email:** Hi {{FirstName}}, I understand that now might not be the right time for {{Company}}. When would be a better time to reconnect about how we can help you achieve [specific outcome]? I’ll set a reminder to reach out then. In the meantime, if anything changes, please don’t hesitate to contact me. Best, [Your Name] [Your Position] [Your Contact Information]

# Segmenting Lead lists # Segmenting Lead lists --- While personalisation with {{FirstName}} and {{CompanyName}} is a great start, the real game-changer lies in advanced segmentation. This is the strategy that will set you apart from 99% of your competitors. # What Is Segmentation? Most people approach cold email campaigns with a one-size-fits-all mindset. They blast a generic message to every lead on their list, regardless of their job title, country, time zone, or cultural differences. This approach results in a low response rate and leaves you wondering what went wrong. Segmentation is the act of splitting your lead lists into smaller, more focused lists based on specific criteria. ## **Why Should You Segment Your Email List?** 1. **Increased Relevance:** Messages resonate more with prospects, as they’re FAR more relevant and make them feel understood, valued and actually spoken to. 2. **Higher Engagement:** Segmented emails see higher reply and positive reply rates. 3. **Better Conversion Rates:** Personalised emails based on segmentation can lead to a higher conversion rate. # **How to Segment Your Lead Lists:** ### **1. Segment By Country:** - **Purpose:** To address cultural nuances, time zones, currencies, idioms and resonate more with prospects - **Technique:** Segment your list based on one country at a time. - **Example:** For a UK-based company, reference local industry events or use British idioms. For a US-based company, focus on challenges specific to the American market. And you should speak differently to someone from the US than you do the UK. ### **2. Segment By Job Title:** - **Purpose:** Directly address the unique challenges and responsibilities associated with specific job titles. - **Technique:** Segment your list by job titles, tailoring your message to the specific needs and goals of each role. - **Example:** A CEO is interested in overall business growth strategies, while a Marketing Director will focus more on campaigns and lead generation techniques and tools. # Segmenting Campaigns Walkthrough This video will walk you through how to create segmented campaigns and how to adjust your messaging in your emails: [https://youtu.be/US09Dc8Ln-U](https://youtu.be/US09Dc8Ln-U) # How To Make Creating Segmented Campaigns 10X Easier **In order to create segmented campaigns that perform you need to make sure you do the following to your lead sheet:** 1. Check there are no blank First Names 2. Check there are no blank company names so you can include {{CompanyName}} in your emails and subject lines 3. Check there are no duplicate emails or blank emails 4. Format the company names so that when you include {{CompanyName}} in your email it looks like it was written by you and is correct 5. Segment by country by creating a filter and manually removing the leads that belong to the specific countries to a separate tab 6. Segment by job title by going through and filtering out every job title and every variation of that title and putting those into separate tabs ### **The Positive:** Doing this really does set you apart from every one else ### **The Problem:** It can take quite a lot of time and effort on your part or you need to train a VA to do it and pay them ### The Solution:
🎯 **Use 👉 www.leadformatter.com**
# What is Lead Formatter and why use it? --- Lead Formatter is a Google Sheets Extension that automatically formats your lead lists in less than 30 seconds making them ready to be plugged into your email sending software ❌ No hours spent manually doing sheets ❌ No money wasted on VAs ❌ No more headaches because of mistakes - **Lead Formatter Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/21c2211b18cd4b39ad45b38738d2f906?sid=7ee02de7-40d6-4e0d-ae34-d8d1b6c930c1](https://www.loom.com/share/21c2211b18cd4b39ad45b38738d2f906?sid=7ee02de7-40d6-4e0d-ae34-d8d1b6c930c1) **Lead Formatter makes sure:** - There are no blank first names so you can use {{FirstName}} in your emails - There are no blank company names so you can use {{CompanyName}} in your emails - There are no blank or duplicate emails meaning you spend less when validating your lists - That all company names are formatted exactly how they should be so you can use {{CompanyName}} in your emails - You can segment by country in seconds and it creates new tabs for each country - Segments all leads by job titles into their own dedicated tab, it segments into the following job titles: Business owners, Marketing, Sales, Finance, HR, Operations and Other ## More ways to segment [https://www.loom.com/share/dc85147588c642ca81524d5ea20cd114?sid=0b39e9af-699f-461c-b32b-714403477d60](https://www.loom.com/share/dc85147588c642ca81524d5ea20cd114?sid=0b39e9af-699f-461c-b32b-714403477d60) ← Previous Add link here Next → Add link here On this page

### How To Scrape LinkedIn Using ClientScraper 1. Create a Sales Nav search Click [**HERE**](https://www.linkedin.com/sales/search/people?query=(recentSearchParam%3A(id%3A1460024506%2CdoLogHistory%3Atrue)%2Cfilters%3AList((type%3AREGION%2Cvalues%3AList((id%3A103644278%2Ctext%3AUnited%2520States%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)))%2C(type%3ACOMPANY_HEADQUARTERS%2Cvalues%3AList((id%3A103644278%2Ctext%3AUnited%2520States%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)))%2C(type%3ATITLE%2Cvalues%3AList((text%3A%2522CEO%2522%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(text%3A%2522founder%2522%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(text%3A%2522owner%2522%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(text%3A%2522chief%2520executive%2520officer%2522%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(text%3A%2522partner%2522%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(text%3Aassistant%2CselectionType%3AEXCLUDED)%2C(text%3Aadvisor%2CselectionType%3AEXCLUDED)%2C(text%3Acoach%2CselectionType%3AEXCLUDED)%2C(text%3Amentor%2CselectionType%3AEXCLUDED)%2C(text%3Aexecutive%2520assistant%2CselectionType%3AEXCLUDED))%2CselectedSubFilter%3ACURRENT)%2C(type%3ASENIORITY_LEVEL%2Cvalues%3AList((id%3A10%2Ctext%3AOwner%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(id%3A9%2Ctext%3APartner%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(id%3A8%2Ctext%3ACXO%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)))%2C(type%3ALEAD_HIGHLIGHTS%2Cvalues%3AList((id%3ARPC%2Ctext%3AChanged%2520jobs%2520in%2520last%252090%2520days%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(id%3ARPOL%2Ctext%3APosted%2520on%2520linkedin%2520in%252030%2520days%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)))%2C(type%3ACOMPANY_HEADCOUNT%2Cvalues%3AList((id%3AA%2Ctext%3ASelf-employed%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(id%3AB%2Ctext%3A1-10%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(id%3AC%2Ctext%3A11-50%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)%2C(id%3AD%2Ctext%3A51-200%2CselectionType%3AINCLUDED)))))&sessionId=joqBqhz8RM%2BgMKjE9%2FHU5A%3D%3D&viewAllFilters=true) to see an example search [https://www.loom.com/share/b11be4e566a34b3daa14732000581458](https://www.loom.com/share/b11be4e566a34b3daa14732000581458) ### **READ THIS 👇** - **Understanding Boolean** Boolean search is a search on LinkedIn that uses logical operators. It uses specific logic using 5 key operators. In plain English is simply allows you to narrow down your searches and get more specific results. **The 5 elements of the syntax are:** --- 1. **OR** You can type “OR” between two words of your search and you should receive the results from both keywords. Example: You can do a search for: “CEO” OR “Owner” OR “Partner”. You should get a list of people who are either a CEO, owner or partner of a business 1. **NOT** Typing “NOT” (all caps) before the searched keyword will exclude any profiles that include it from the results. You can also use the minus sign (-) instead of NOT to achieve the same result. Example: “Marketing Manager” NOT “sales”. This type of search will give you a much more accurate list of people related to marketing excluding people that are in sales 1. **Parenthesis searches** Using parentheses in your search query, you tell the search engine how to handle the order of importance. This is essential when combining AND and OR queries. Example: (“CEO” AND “Managing Director) OR “Owner”. When you do this type of search, it will include only those profiles that mention both “CEO” and “Managing Director” at the same time, or those that mention just “Owner” 1. **AND** In order for this operator to work, you have to type it in all caps - “AND”. Typing it in lower case won’t work. Example: “Sales Manager” AND “Insurance”. This will bring up results for people that are sales managers in the insurance industry 1. **Quotation Marks** Using quotation “ “ marks around certain phrases will show you only results that exactly match your query. Example: “Business Development Manager”. Typing it this way will only give you search results for people that have a business development manager position mentioned on their profile. - **A list of potential titles for Sales Nav** - General "CEO" OR "CMO" OR "COO" OR "ceo" OR "cmo" OR "coo" OR "Chief Operating Officer" OR "Chief Executive Officer" OR "Chief Marketing Officer" OR "VP of sales" OR "VP of sales and marketing" OR "VP of marketing" OR "founder" OR "Owner" OR "President" OR "President and CEO" OR "Owner and CEO" OR "Owner and Managing Director" OR "Owner and Director" OR "Owner and General Manager" OR "Head of marketing" OR "Head of operations" OR "Head of sales" OR "chief executive" OR "co-founder" OR "co-owner" OR "marketing director" OR "director of marketing" OR "sales director" - SaaS "brand director" OR "head of brand" OR "head of branding" OR "brand manager" OR "marketing technology lead" OR "marketing tech lead" OR "marketing director" OR "head of marketing" OR brand portfolio manager" OR "director of business development" OR "brand reputation manager" OR "senior brand manager" OR "CTO" OR "CMO" OR "cto" OR "cmo" OR "strategy director" OR "head of strategy" OR "director of strategy" OR "business development director" OR "business strategy director" OR "head of loyalty" OR "head of CRM" OR "chief marketing officer" OR "CCO" OR "cco" OR "cbo" OR "chief commercial officer" OR "chief data officer" OR "CDO" OR "head of data" OR "marketing technology manager" OR "head of technology" OR "sales manager" OR "ceo" OR "founder" OR "co-founder" OR "president" OR "vp of sales" OR "vp of marketing" OR "global sales manager" OR "chief operating officer" OR "chief executive officer" OR "ceo" OR "vp of sales and marketing" OR "owner and managing director" OR "co-owner" ### C. Ocean.io Find look-a-like companies of your clients or your clients’ case studies using [**Ocean**](https://www.ocean.io/) ### What is Ocean and why use it? Ocean is a tool that allows you to find look-a-likes of companies very easily **For when:** 1. You have a great client already and you want to find other companies that are similar to them so you can reach out and offer your services and use your client as the social proof to get them to book a call 2. You just started working with a new lead gen client and to make it easier to find similar prospects to reach out to you find look-a-like companies of all of their case study companies ### How to scrape Ocean for Free
⚠️ Ocean isn’t cheap. **It costs about €1,500 set up + €275/month.** Meaning it's too expensive for most people. But, we have found a way for you to scrape it for free.
[https://www.loom.com/share/c931075862b0445ba849fbc1b78cecdd?sid=a21ca30e-87c6-469d-bd8c-b840093c5a49](https://www.loom.com/share/c931075862b0445ba849fbc1b78cecdd?sid=a21ca30e-87c6-469d-bd8c-b840093c5a49) ### Where to paste the script [https://www.loom.com/share/110eea8c10ad422394e81dd2c0139e54?sid=e6dfd1fb-a523-4823-8259-fe4d8608719a](https://www.loom.com/share/110eea8c10ad422394e81dd2c0139e54?sid=e6dfd1fb-a523-4823-8259-fe4d8608719a) ### Script --- ```jsx function onOpen() { var ui = SpreadsheetApp.getUi(); ui.createMenu('Format Ocean Data') .addItem('Format and Clean Data', 'formatAndCleanData') .addToUi(); } function formatAndCleanData() { var sheet = SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet().getActiveSheet(); var range = sheet.getDataRange(); var values = range.getValues(); var output = []; values.forEach(function(row, index) { if (index === 0) { // Set the header row for the first two columns output.push(['Website', 'Company Name']); } else { var website = row[0] ? extractDomain(row[0]) : ''; if (website) { // Check if website is not empty // Use the extracted domain as the website in column A var companyName = website.split('.')[0]; // Extracts the company name from the domain output.push([website, companyName]); // Corrected: website now contains the domain name } } }); // Clear the original data and write the new data range.clearContent(); sheet.getRange(1, 1, output.length, 2).setValues(output); // Apply formatting to headers var headerRange = sheet.getRange(1, 1, 1, 2); headerRange.setBackground('orange').setFontWeight('bold'); } function extractDomain(url) { var match = url.match(/https?:\/\/cdn\.ocean\.io\/companies-logos-v1\/([^\/]+)\//); return match ? match[1] : ''; } ``` ## 7. # Understanding List Building

# Understanding List Building

---

# **What is Lead Scraping?**

Lead scraping is the process of using tools to gather information about potential customers who might be interested in what you're offering. Think of it as a smart way to find out who might need your products or services without having to meet them face-to-face.

# **Why is it Important?**

With lead scraping, you can:

- Reach the right people quickly.
- Save time and effort, letting the tools do most of the work.
- Improve your sales by targeting those most likely to buy.

# **Best Practices**

1. Know Your Target Audience:
2. Use the Right Tools
3. Respect Privacy Laws
4. Keep Data Clean and Updated
5. Learn Continuously

cold-email/list-building/validating-lead.content:
# Validating Leads # Validating Leads --- # **Why Validate Email Addresses?** Validating email addresses before sending to them is crucial. It allows us to make sure we’re sending to ACTIVE and VALID email addresses Meaning they belong to real users This not only improves the deliverability of our emails but also protects our sender reputation # **The Importance of Low Bounce Rates** One of the main reasons for validating emails is to keep bounce rates low. Bounces happen when emails are undeliverable, either because the address doesn't exist or the recipient's inbox is full High bounce rates can negatively affect our sender reputation and may even lead to our emails being marked as spam ## **Aim for Below 2% Bounce Rate** Ideally, we aim to keep bounce rates below 2%. If you see campaigns go above 2% Pause them and re-verify the list ## **Sending to Validated Email Addresses Only**
⚠️ We only send to VALID email addresses
Sending emails to invalid, catch-all or non-existent addresses ruins our deliverability ## MillionVerifier We recommend [MillionVerifier](https://www.millionverifier.com/) to validate email addresses - **MillionVerifier Walkthrough Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/ae83dfdf3e8c459aa9dfc13127dc722a?source=embed_watch_on_loom_cta](https://www.loom.com/share/ae83dfdf3e8c459aa9dfc13127dc722a?source=embed_watch_on_loom_cta) # **Dealing with Catch-All Emails** Catch-all emails are email addresses that accept emails sent to any random or misspelled address within a domain. While convenient for recipients, catch-all emails can result in higher bounce rates and lower deliverability. Meaning we DON’T send to them ## **Validating Catch-Alls** We do not recommend sending emails to catch-all and unknown leads. Very them with [Scrubby](http://scrubby.io). It is expensive but if you can afford it, it will give you more valid emails to send to. Scrubby helps identify and filter out catch-all and invalid addresses, ensuring that our email campaigns are more targeted and effective
🎁 **Use Scrubby To Verify Catch-All Emails (get 30% off code: CCG30)**
- **Scrubby Walkthrough Video** [https://www.loom.com/share/91db6dd852164be4859b70e672c73ee4](https://www.loom.com/share/91db6dd852164be4859b70e672c73ee4) ← Previous Add link here Next → Add link here On this page

# How To Stand Out From Everyone Else Selling Lead Gen

# How To Stand Out From Everyone Else Selling Lead Gen

---

The thing with selling lead gen is that many companies have received so much outreach over the years that they may instantly class your outreach as “one of those”. 

The problem isn’t that the market is saturated or leadgen is “dead”, it's that we need to find more innovative ways of differentiating our services so we can crack the market. 

## How To Stand Out

[https://www.loom.com/share/5169c4af09ae43f7a8105fb09c2cb542?sid=2f60b0e4-dd16-4119-8148-d1ca836a693b](https://www.loom.com/share/5169c4af09ae43f7a8105fb09c2cb542?sid=2f60b0e4-dd16-4119-8148-d1ca836a693b)

cold-email/presenting-your-offer/value-a.content:
# “Value add” Angle

# “Value add” Angle

---

This is what we call **the “Value Add” angle**. This angle does as it says and leads with value. The purpose of the approach when leading with value is to:

1. Show competence + expertise + trustworthiness
2. Provide value so they feel they ‘owe’ you something 

An easy example of this is offering free leads to prospects

# **Email Template**

**Subject: [Value add] for {CompanyName}**

![Screenshot 2024-02-05 at 09.02.12.png](%E2%80%9CValue%20add%E2%80%9D%20Angle/Screenshot_2024-02-05_at_09.02.12.png)

# **Fulfilling this angle**

### **Step 1**

A prospects responds. Use this as an opportunity to review their website to qualify them based off of your ICP. If they qualify move onto step 2.

**Step 2**

DO NOT SEND LEADS WITHOUT GETTING ON A CALL WITH A PROSPECT

TELL THEM YOU NEED A CALL TO GO OVER THEIR ICP IN DETAIL TO PROVIDE BEST LEADS POSSIBLE

IF THEY DON’T WANT A CALL THEY DON’T GET THE LEADS

### **Step 3**

Put the companies from their case studies into Apollo and identify their keywords

![gif.gif](%E2%80%9CValue%20add%E2%80%9D%20Angle/gif.gif)

### **Step 4**

Create a new search based off of these key words, scrape them and put them into a Google Sheet.

### **Step 5**

Reply to prospect delivering leads and say that as part of that search you have X number of more leads available. Explain you could also scrape these and email them on behalf of the company to generate meetings on a performance basis - push the call

It connects employers with experienced professionals in the U.S. and Canada looking for $100k+ positions across a range of industries. | | **FlexJobs** | FlexJobs advertises legitimate telecommuting, remote, and flexible jobs around the world that have been vetted by the FlexJobs team. | | **Nexxt** | Nexxt is a general job board that has been operating since 1998 and powers a host of niche job sites for highly targeted recruitment efforts. | | **We Work Remotely** | We Work Remotely is the most popular job board for startups looking to hire remote workers. The main job type is software development but they are also an excellent source for designers, marketers, and other startup roles. | | **Dice** | Dice focuses on information technology, engineering professionals, and high-level tech positions, and features thousands of tech job listings. | | **ClearanceJobs** | ClearanceJobs is a job board for employers looking to connect with job seekers who have a security clearance. It claims to be “the largest career network for professionals with federal government security clearance.” | | **Wellfound** | Wellfound is a valuable resource for startup companies looking to scale their operations and hire remote talent in tech, software development, data science, finance, and more. | - **Using Octoparse to scrape job boards** [https://www.loom.com/share/834b77551de34e1dabe9bea09c6d035e?sid=12971be5-bcc6-4c97-ae4c-b5983807fe98](https://www.loom.com/share/834b77551de34e1dabe9bea09c6d035e?sid=12971be5-bcc6-4c97-ae4c-b5983807fe98) ### Linkedin Jobs LinkedIn Jobs is a massive database of jobs that allow us to find companies with a budget looking to solve a problem and find ways to contact them all in one platform **Tools To Use To Scrape LinkedIn Jobs** - [**Clay**](https://www.notion.so/ed710428d58344b29726cc2e36afa9d4?pvs=21) - [**Apify**](https://www.notion.so/ed710428d58344b29726cc2e36afa9d4?pvs=21) We recommend using Clay to scrape LinkedIn jobs - How To Scrape LinkedIn Jobs Using Clay [https://www.loom.com/share/0c481049b815487892243b95dffb7a13?sid=ce4c4ae1-2edf-4564-aac7-5969ae66f252](https://www.loom.com/share/0c481049b815487892243b95dffb7a13?sid=ce4c4ae1-2edf-4564-aac7-5969ae66f252) ### Approach once you’ve scraped companies hiring for your services **Email Template:**

cold-email/running-improving-campaigns/m.content:
# Market Research # Market Research and Targeting --- THE NO.1 AREA WHERE MOST PEOPLE CAN INSTANTLY IMPROVE THEIR CAMPAIGNS IS THROUGH BETTER UNDERSTANDING OF THE MARKET AND THEIR TARGETING Most people think they have a good idea of the ICP and target market. They haven’t to the degree they need to. ## The Importance Of Market Research Understanding your target market's pain points is the most important part in crafting compelling email campaigns. THE MOST IMPORTANT Market research and understanding your target market is the single biggest difference between getting great results and no results It’s exactly the reason why people find it easy to book calls for lead gen but struggle to book calls for their clients The lead gen market research was already done for them ## How To Use **ChatGPT To Do Market Research For You** We’re going to be using GO Lead Generation as the example company - A lead generation agency that is targeting B2B SaaS companies based in the US to make the point hit home more for you You would amend these sections to match your requirements ### **1. Identify Target Industry:** > **Prompt:** "We want to conduct market research on [Target Market] in order to better sell them our [Your Service]. Our ICP is [Map out your ICP]. Please respond “OK” to confirm you have understood." > ![Screenshot 2023-11-02 at 13.23.10.png](Market%20Research/Screenshot_2023-11-02_at_13.23.10.png) ### **2. Identify Job Titles:** > **Prompt:** "Identify the job titles responsible for [outcome your offer provides] within [target industry]." > ![Screenshot 2023-11-02 at 13.25.41.png](Market%20Research/Screenshot_2023-11-02_at_13.25.41.png) ### **3. Job Titles Supervision:** **Finding Decision Makers:** > **Prompt:** "Determine the roles that would most likely have the authority to make a decision on working with us.” > ![Screenshot 2023-11-02 at 13.29.17.png](Market%20Research/Screenshot_2023-11-02_at_13.29.17.png) ### **4. Identifying Pain Points:** > **Prompt:** "Create a table where column 1 lists the job titles of the decision-makers. Create a new column and please list the common pain points faced by each job title as it relates to [Your Service’s Outcome]." > ![Screenshot 2023-11-02 at 13.33.48.png](Market%20Research/Screenshot_2023-11-02_at_13.33.48.png) ### **5. Ranking Pain Points:** > **Prompt:** "In column 2 please rank these pain points based on their likely impact on the relevant job titles from 1-10. 1 being very low impact and 10 being extremely high.” > ![Screenshot 2023-11-02 at 13.36.24.png](Market%20Research/Screenshot_2023-11-02_at_13.36.24.png) ### **6. Understanding Solutions Previously Tried:** > **Prompt:** “In a fourth column, List the typical solutions each job title will have tried to overcome the pain points in column 2.” > ![Screenshot 2023-11-02 at 13.37.58.png](Market%20Research/Screenshot_2023-11-02_at_13.37.58.png) ### **7. Using Research to Provide Solutions:** > **Prompt:** "Here’s [your company's] website text. Please create a fifth column that outlines how we can help the relevant job titles from column 1 solve the pain points in column 2." > ![Screenshot 2023-11-02 at 13.42.38.png](Market%20Research/Screenshot_2023-11-02_at_13.42.38.png) ## **Applying Research to Email Templates** Use [**LeadFormatter**](http://leadformatter.com) to segment your email lists and format data points to be able to use this email template **Example:** Hi {{FirstName}}, Are you looking to align sales and marketing, to drive sustainable growth, whilst balancing investments at {{CompanyName}}? Often we speak to SaaS companies that have tried strategic planning, CRMs and lead generation but these still didn’t solve their problem. [Your offer] CTA Thanks, [Your Name]
💡 Not only does this speak directly to each prospect knowing their role, pain points and solutions they’ve likely tried, but having personal, relevant and hard-hitting emails that people can’t help but reply to
## Other Ways To Conduct Market Research Here are some other ways to conduct market research - **Surveys and questionnaires:** In exchange for a value add/lead magnet - **Interviews with industry experts:** Reach out to prospects and ask if can interview them to gain their insights for a white-paper you’re creating and you will share the insights gathered with them - **Social media and forums** - **Customer feedback:** Assess customer feedback of your target market ← Previous [Back](/resources/running-improving-campaigns-23f0565975a9488b95c3e96ebb32a3bc) Next → [Running Outreach](/resources/running-outreach-142f6d94771f81b983a4dcdda6668975) On this page

# Running Outreach - Control panel ⚡️ # Running Outreach --- # 1. Ensure Deliverability
⚠️ Make sure to go through **everything** on this page: [Deliverability](/resources/deliverability-d9032c1a093b4bd18b7be85e24f1f109)
# **2. Tracking and Monitoring Campaign Performance** You should track your campaign performance directly from within your ESP. So inside Instantly or Smartlead Use their built-in campaign analytics ## Key Metrics To Track Campaign Performance Metrics we recommend you track especially when not tracking open rate | **Metric** | | --- | | **Reply rate** | | **Overall Positive Reply Rate** | | **Positive Reply Rate %** | | **Meeting Booked Rate** | | **Positive Reply to Meeting Conversion Rate** | | **Call Show-up rate** | | **Bounce rate** | | **Emails sent per meetings booked** | ## **Target Performance Metrics** | **Metric** | **Target** | | --- | --- | | **Reply rate** | **5%** | | **Overall positive Reply rate** | **2%** | | **Positive reply as % of replies** | **40%** | | **Meeting booked rate** | **1.8% (industry average 0.5 - 1%)** | | **Positive Reply to Meeting Conversion Rate** | **45%** | | **Call show-up rate** | **80%** | | **Bounce rate** | **<2%** | ### Template Campaign Performance Tracking Spreadsheet
💡 Spreadsheet Link - [**Access the Outreach Performance Spreadsheet**](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1QyiPvUeIOFZmjw_n09ntMIG-weX6czuiMymglQomWo0/edit#gid=0)
The spreadsheet is structured to provide a clear view of your campaign's core metrics: - **Calls Booked**: Track the number of potential clients who've agreed to a meeting - **Calls Showed**: Monitor the actual number of meetings held - **Show Rate %**: This key metric offers insights into the effectiveness of your confirmation and reminder process. Aiming for an 80% or higher show rate is a strong benchmark - **Clients Closed**: The ultimate goal - how many of these meetings turn into paying clients - **Closure Rate %**: Understanding this percentage helps refine your sales pitch and meeting approach to improve conversion rates. --- Input your campaign data into the spreadsheet to not only track campaigns but to also: - **Determine the Optimal Email Volume**: Calculate the number of emails needed to book a call, ensuring you're focusing efforts efficiently. - **Identify Conversion Trends**: Spot patterns in successful conversions to refine your targeting and messaging. - **Monitor Campaign Health**: Quick glance metrics like show rate and closure rate offer immediate feedback on your campaign's effectiveness. ### **Review and Adjust Regularly** The key to successful campaign management is not just in tracking but in regularly reviewing the data and making necessary adjustments ## Campaign FAQs
💡 Checkout our Campaign FAQs section [Campaign FAQs](../../Key%20Resources/Frequently%20Asked%20Questions%20(FAQs)/Campaign%20FAQs%20d574dd8203b2493fb80aee18db8d814e.md)

fulfilment/client-communications-best-pr.title: Client Communications Best Practices
fulfilment/client-communications-best-pr.content:
# Client Communications Best Practices # Client Communications Best Practices --- ### **1. Over communicate** When you’re not communicating, clients can be left wondering what you’re doing or can easily assume you’re doing nothing Don’t let this happen Over communication makes sure clients are aware of your efforts and progress, even when not immediately visible **We’re talking DAILY updates:** - Just got this response - This person said this - This many interested leads - Open rate was this so we’re testing this ### **2. Update Them Regularly** **Stat:** Agencies that provide weekly updates see a 30% higher retention rate **In addition to your daily updates make sure you have weekly check-ins** A brief call or email highlighting the week’s efforts, successes, and next steps ### 3. Be Transparent Be honest always. ### **4. Schedule Regular Calls** Try and make sure you have at least two calls with clients every month ### **5. Ask for Feedback** Regularly ask clients what you could do better. This not only provides valuable insights for service improvement but also makes clients feel valued and heard
💡 Use this to gather feedback from your clients: [Client Feedback Form](Client%20Communications%20Best%20Practices/Client_Feedback_Form_Template.docx)
### **6. Be Proactive** Don’t wait for clients to ask for updates. Be Proactive and share insights, trends, and recommendations Google just announced changes to their email delivery, tell them about it first and present how if at all it effects what you’re doing. Whenever you bring bad new or problems, follow up with what you’re planning to do to fix it. ← Previous [Fulfilment Automations](/resources/fulfilment-automations-ddb08874403448f2bf829fb6e0b1e1b9) Next → [Reporting & Client Communication Automations](/resources/reporting-and-client-communication-automations-b219889868644f60911a120f574e7e46) On this page

fulfilment/fulfillment-best-practices-fo.content:
# Fulfillment Best Practices for Lead Gen Agencies

# Fulfillment Best Practices for Lead Gen Agencies

## **1. Client Onboarding**

First impressions are key. Start the relationship with a great onboarding experience. Only then can you look to take on more clients and scale. 

[Onboarding](/resources/onboarding-9e80aad0d2c24ecf85f8a83971f1b2bd)

## **2. Ready-to-Go Generic Domains**

While you're warming up your client's specific domains, have some generic ones warmed and ready to start outreach as soon as they sign. Think of it as your head start.

**Generic domain example:** [emailconnector.com](http://emailconnector.com/)

**Why?** No waiting around. You start showing value from the start and kill their buyers remorse straight away. 

## **3. Dream Client Outreach**

Ask your clients who their dream clients are.

Then, hit those high-priority prospects with emails tailored just for them (send them manually). 

If you land a meeting with one of these companies you have HUGE leverage

**Why It’s Smart:** You’re showing you’re out to get results and are ballsy enough to go after the big guys

## 4. Re-engagement Campaigns

To secure quick wins for a new client, re-activate warm leads from their CRM—those who showed interest previously, but didn’t convert.

Request this list by and focus on leads who interacted within the last 3-12 months for the best chance of conversion. 

Once you have the list, run a personalized email sequence to re-engage them and drive bookings or purchases. This strategy delivers fast results and builds trust with the client.

## **5. Know Their Offer Inside Out**

You’ve got to dive deep into what your client's offering – as if it's your own offer. 

The NO 1 reason people fail to get results for clients is because they don’t know their offer well enough 

IT IS THAT SIMPLE

The reason you can book calls for yourself is because you know your offer inside out and can communicate it

Do that with your clients and you’ll get great results

fulfilment/increasing-client-retention-9.title: Increasing Client Retention
fulfilment/increasing-client-retention-9.content:
# Increasing Client Retention

# Increasing Client Retention

---

Everything we map out inside the resources will lead to increased client retention.

There is no shiny object that once you start doing that thing, client retention increase. Otherwise, everyone would do that thing and client retention wouldn’t be an issue 

Provide the most value possible to your clients and follow these step.

# The 4 Pillars Of Client Retention

There are really just 4 pillars to client retention that actually move the needle

We go through each one below to help you understand the approach you should take (which should be different for each client) when looking to maximise client retention

### **1. Position Yourself as an Indispensable Partner**

By solving their problems, providing value and by constantly communicating you can position yourself as an indispensable (to some degree) partner

**Stat:** Agencies that engage in strategic advising report a 25% higher retention rate compared to those that don't 

What does this mean? Give advice on things that you’re an expert in outside the scope of what they’re paying for 

Go above and beyond

### **2. Personalise Client Interactions**

Speak to clients how they want to be spoken to, via the channels they like to use for instance if a client prefers email or phone over Slack, make that the comms channel.

### **3. Deliver and Communicate Value Constantly**

Every opportunity you can provide clients with updates on the value you’re providing

Show them where your service has taken them

Provide weekly performance reports with clear metrics that demonstrate the value of your services. Highlight successes, address areas of improvement, and outline plans for the following week.

### **4. Invest in Client Education**

Host webinars, workshops, or create resource libraries to educate clients on lead generation trends, tools, and strategies

Educated clients are more likely to understand the value of your work and stay engaged

**Stat:** Agencies that provide regular educational content to clients have a 35% higher retention rate
